 `./worktrees/298-tap-forgiveness/src/components/answer-game/AnswerGame/AnswerGame.flows.mdx`

```text
1	import { Meta } from '@storybook/blocks';
2	
3	<Meta title="AnswerGame/Flows" />
4	
5	# AnswerGame — Event Flows
6	
7	> Source: `src/components/answer-game/`
8	>
9	> Each diagram shows the sequence of dispatches and effects triggered by a user action.
10	> Update this file when adding new dispatch chains or changing existing ones.
11	> Run `/update-architecture-docs` for guided update prompts.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## 1. Tile Placement
16	
17	Bank tile click/tap or keyboard character press.
18	
19	```mermaid
20	sequenceDiagram
21	  actor User
22	  participant Hook as useTileEvaluation
23	  participant Reducer as answerGameReducer
24	  participant Effect as useSlotBehavior (effect)
25	
26	  User->>Hook: click bank tile / press key
27	  Hook->>Reducer: PLACE_TILE { tileId, zoneIndex }
28	  Reducer-->>Reducer: move tile from bankTileIds → zones[zoneIndex].placedTileId
29	  Reducer-->>Reducer: evaluate: all zones correct?
30	  alt all zones correct
31	    Reducer-->>Effect: phase = 'round-complete'
32	    Effect->>Effect: triggerPop animation
33	  else mismatch (wrongTileBehavior = 'reject')
34	    Reducer-->>Reducer: tile not placed, bankTileIds unchanged
35	  else mismatch (wrongTileBehavior = 'lock-manual' or 'lock-auto-eject')
36	    Reducer-->>Reducer: zones[zoneIndex].isWrong = true, isLocked = true
37	    Effect->>Effect: triggerShake animation
38	  end
39	```
40	
41	### Tap / click via `useAutoNextSlot` (ordered slots)
42	
43	Bank **tap** (not drag) goes through `useDraggableTile.handleClick` → `useAutoNextSlot.placeInNextSlot`:
44	
45	1. **`pendingPlacements`** (module-scoped write-ahead log) records `{ tileId, zoneIndex }` before React applies `PLACE_TILE`, so rapid taps see prior claims synchronously.
46	2. Entries are **drained** when `zones[entry.zoneIndex].placedTileId === entry.tileId`. The queue is **cleared** on round transitions (`roundIndex` / `zones.length` changes in `useEffect`).
47	3. **Pre-validation**: for `reject` and `lock-auto-eject`, wrong tiles never call `placeTile`; `handleClick` applies bank shake + wrong sound + **`REJECT_TAP`** + `game:evaluate` (with `expected`). For **`lock-manual`**, there is no bank pre-validation — wrong tiles still flow through `PLACE_TILE` into the slot.
48	
49	### `REJECT_TAP`
50	
51	Reducer increments **`retryCount`** only (no zone or bank mutation). Dispatched from **`handleClick`** on bank-side rejection, and from **`useTileEvaluation.placeTile`** for drag-drop wrong tiles when `wrongTileBehavior === 'reject'`.
52	
53	Wrong bank feedback (**shake + wrong colors**) uses **`flashBankTileRejectFeedback`** so the tile matches **`Slot`** wrong styling (`border-2` equivalent and **`--skin-wrong-*`** tokens). **Touch** drops invoke it from **`useDraggableTile`** (after `requestAnimationFrame`). **Pragmatic drag-and-drop** bank→slot drops invoke it from **`useSlotBehavior.handleDrop`** when `placeTile` returns incorrect in **`reject`** mode (same helper, resolved via **`data-tile-bank-hole`**).
54	
55	---
56	
57	## 2. Wrong Tile Auto-Eject
58	
59	Only when `config.wrongTileBehavior === 'lock-auto-eject'`.
60	
61	```mermaid
62	sequenceDiagram
63	  participant Reducer as answerGameReducer
64	  participant Effect as useSlotBehavior (effect)
65	
66	  Reducer-->>Effect: zones[zoneIndex].isWrong = true
67	  Effect->>Effect: triggerShake animation (immediate)
68	  Effect->>Effect: wait 350ms
69	  Effect->>Effect: triggerEjectReturn animation
70	  Effect->>Effect: wait 1000ms
71	  Effect->>Reducer: EJECT_TILE { zoneIndex }
72	  Reducer-->>Reducer: zones[zoneIndex].placedTileId = null, isWrong = false, isLocked = false
73	  Reducer-->>Reducer: tile id returned to bankTileIds
74	```
75	
76	---
77	
78	## 3. Drag and Drop
79	
80	Dragging a bank tile or slot tile onto a slot.
81	
82	```mermaid
83	flowchart TD
84	  A([User starts drag]) --> B[SET_DRAG_ACTIVE tileId]
85	  B --> C{Drag over slot?}
86	  C -- enter --> D[SET_DRAG_HOVER zoneIndex]
87	  C -- leave --> E[SET_DRAG_HOVER null]
88	  D --> F{Drop?}
89	  F -- bank tile on empty slot --> G[PLACE_TILE tileId zoneIndex]
90	  F -- bank tile on occupied slot --> H[SWAP_TILES or PLACE_TILE]
91	  F -- slot tile on slot --> I[SWAP_TILES fromZone toZone]
92	  F -- slot tile on bank tile hole --> J[SWAP_SLOT_BANK zoneIndex bankTileId]
93	  F -- slot tile on bank container --> K[REMOVE_TILE zoneIndex]
94	  G & H & I & J & K --> L[SET_DRAG_ACTIVE null]
95	  L --> M[SET_DRAG_HOVER null]
96	```
97	
98	**Drag-over bank tile (slot tile being dragged):**
99	
100	```mermaid
101	sequenceDiagram
102	  participant Hook as useSlotTileDrag
103	  participant Reducer as answerGameReducer
104	
105	  Hook->>Reducer: SET_DRAG_HOVER_BANK { tileId: bankTileId }
106	  Note over Hook,Reducer: bank tile shows "hole" preview
107	  Hook->>Reducer: drop → SWAP_SLOT_BANK { zoneIndex, bankTileId }
108	  Hook->>Reducer: SET_DRAG_HOVER_BANK { tileId: null }
109	  Hook->>Reducer: SET_DRAG_ACTIVE { tileId: null }
110	```
111	
112	**Tap forgiveness (micro-drag → tap, touch only):**
113	
114	When a touch drag ends and total displacement from start is below
115	`tapForgivenessThreshold` (configurable per-profile, default 20px)
116	and no slot zone is under the pointer, the gesture is treated as a
117	tap instead of a cancelled drag.
118	
119	```mermaid
120	sequenceDiagram
121	  actor User
122	  participant Hook as useTouchDrag (onPointerUp)
123	  participant Fallback as useDraggableTile (onTapFallback)
124	  participant Reducer as answerGameReducer
125	  participant Auto as useAutoNextSlot
126	
127	  User->>Hook: pointer up (displacement < threshold)
128	  Hook->>Hook: check: no zone under pointer?
129	  Hook->>Fallback: onTapFallback()
130	  Fallback->>Reducer: SET_DRAG_ACTIVE { tileId: null }
131	  Fallback->>Auto: placeInNextSlot(tile.id)
132	  Auto->>Reducer: PLACE_TILE { tileId, zoneIndex: activeSlotIndex }
133	```
134	
135	---
136	
137	## 4. Keyboard and Touch Input
138	
139	Desktop keyboard (`useKeyboardInput`) and mobile hidden input (`useTouchKeyboardInput`).
140	
141	```mermaid
142	sequenceDiagram
143	  actor User
144	  participant KI as useKeyboardInput (global keydown)
145	  participant TE as useTileEvaluation
146	  participant Reducer as answerGameReducer
147	
148	  User->>KI: ArrowLeft / ArrowRight
149	  KI->>Reducer: SET_ACTIVE_SLOT { zoneIndex: next }
150	
151	  User->>KI: Backspace / Delete
152	  KI->>KI: find last filled slot ≤ activeSlotIndex
153	  KI->>Reducer: REMOVE_TILE { zoneIndex: i }
154	
155	  User->>KI: printable character
156	  KI->>KI: find matching tile in bank?
157	  alt tile found in bank
158	    KI->>TE: placeTile(matchingTile.id, activeSlotIndex)
159	    TE->>Reducer: PLACE_TILE { tileId, zoneIndex }
160	  else no match
161	    KI->>TE: typeTile(char, activeSlotIndex)
162	    TE->>Reducer: TYPE_TILE { tileId: "typed-{id}", value: char, zoneIndex }
163	  end
164	```
165	
166	Touch keyboard follows the same path via `useTouchKeyboardInput`, using the hidden
167	`<input>` element's `input` event instead of global `keydown`.
168	
169	---
170	
171	## 5. Level Progression (SortNumbers only)
172	
173	Only when `config.levelMode` is configured.
174	
175	```mermaid
176	stateDiagram-v2
177	  [*] --> playing
178	
179	  playing --> round_complete : all zones correct
180	  round_complete --> playing : ADVANCE_ROUND\n(more rounds in level)
181	  round_complete --> level_complete : ADVANCE_ROUND\n(last round in level)
182	  level_complete --> playing : ADVANCE_LEVEL\n(user clicks Next Level)
183	  level_complete --> game_over : COMPLETE_GAME\n(generateNextLevel returns null\nor maxLevels reached)
184	  playing --> game_over : COMPLETE_GAME\n(no levelMode + last round)
185	```
186	
187	**Dispatch sequence for level transition:**
188	
189	```mermaid
190	sequenceDiagram
191	  participant Component as SortNumbers
192	  participant Reducer as answerGameReducer
193	
194	  Component->>Component: phase = 'level-complete' detected
195	  Component->>Component: user taps "Next Level"
196	  Component->>Component: config.levelMode.generateNextLevel(levelIndex)
197	  alt returns { tiles, zones }
198	    Component->>Reducer: ADVANCE_LEVEL { tiles, zones }
199	    Reducer-->>Reducer: levelIndex++, roundIndex = 0
200	  else returns null
201	    Component->>Reducer: COMPLETE_GAME
202	    Reducer-->>Reducer: phase = 'game-over'
203	  end
204	```
205	
206	---
207	
208	## 6. HUD Auto-Mount
209	
210	`AnswerGame` renders a `ProgressHUDRoot` as the first child of its outer
211	flex container every time a session mounts. No per-game wiring is
212	required.
213	
214	```mermaid
215	sequenceDiagram
216	  participant Game as WordSpell / NumberMatch / SortNumbers
217	  participant AG as AnswerGame
218	  participant Root as ProgressHUDRoot
219	  participant Ctx as AnswerGameStateContext
220	  participant HUD as ProgressHUD (default or skin.ProgressHUD)
221	
222	  Game->>AG: render <AnswerGame config skin>
223	  AG->>Root: mount as first child
224	  Root->>Ctx: read config, phase, roundIndex, levelIndex, isLevelMode
225	  Root->>Root: resolveHudFlags(config.hud, isLevelMode)
226	  alt skin.ProgressHUD defined
227	    Root->>HUD: render skin.ProgressHUD with resolved props
228	  else default
229	    Root->>HUD: render built-in ProgressHUD with resolved props
230	  end
231	```
232	
233	---
234	
235	## 7. Custom Game Delete
236	
237	User deletes the current custom game from the `InstructionsOverlay` settings cog.
238	
239	```mermaid
240	sequenceDiagram
241	  actor User
242	  participant GameBody as GameBody ($gameId.tsx)
243	  participant IO as InstructionsOverlay
244	  participant ACM as AdvancedConfigModal
245	  participant Hook as useCustomGames
246	  participant DB as custom_games (RxDB)
247	  participant Nav as useNavigate
248	
249	  User->>IO: click cog (Configure)
250	  IO->>ACM: open modal (mode = customGame)
251	  User->>ACM: click Delete
252	  ACM->>ACM: open confirmation dialog
253	  User->>ACM: click Delete (confirm)
254	  ACM->>IO: call onDeleteCustomGame(configId)
255	  IO->>GameBody: onDeleteCustomGame(configId) [prop callback into $gameId.tsx]
256	  GameBody->>Hook: remove(id)
257	  Hook->>DB: doc.remove()
258	  GameBody->>Nav: navigate({ search: prev => ({ ...prev, configId: undefined }) })
259	  ACM->>ACM: close confirmation dialog + modal
260	```
261	
262	---
263	
264	## 8. Round Progression
265	
266	> **Status: planned — not yet fully implemented.**
267	> The phase transition to `'round-complete'` is implemented. The 750ms delay before
268	> `ADVANCE_ROUND` is implemented in `WordSpell`, `NumberMatch`, and `SortNumbers`.
269	> A shared round-progression hook is not yet extracted.
270	
271	```mermaid
272	sequenceDiagram
273	  participant Reducer as answerGameReducer
274	  participant Component as WordSpell / NumberMatch / SortNumbers
275	  participant Sound as sound system
276	
277	  Reducer-->>Component: phase = 'round-complete'
278	  Component->>Component: wait for sound ready signal
279	  Component->>Component: wait 750ms (confetti animation)
280	  alt more rounds remain
281	    Component->>Reducer: ADVANCE_ROUND { tiles, zones }
282	    Reducer-->>Reducer: roundIndex++, zones cleared, bankTileIds reset
283	  else last round
284	    Component->>Reducer: COMPLETE_GAME
285	    Reducer-->>Reducer: phase = 'game-over'
286	  end
287	```
288	
289	---
290	
291	## 9. Session Resume (fresh mount vs. draft restore)
292	
293	`AnswerGameProvider`'s mount effect routes every lifecycle change through
294	the reducer. On mount, and whenever `initialState` or `config.initialTiles/Zones`
295	change, exactly one of `RESUME_ROUND` or `INIT_ROUND` is dispatched.
296	
297	`RESUME_ROUND` preserves draft progress (`roundIndex`, `retryCount`,
298	`levelIndex`), so a resumed mid-round session never resets to round 0 —
299	that was the pre-#139 failure mode the early-return guard was masking.
300	
301	`useAnswerGameDraftSync` runs in parallel: it writes every state change
302	back to `session_history_index.draftState` (debounced 500 ms) so the
303	next mount has a fresh draft to hand to `RESUME_ROUND`.
304	
305	```mermaid
306	flowchart TD
307	  Mount([AnswerGameProvider mounts or props change]) --> HasDraft{initialState present?}
308	
309	  HasDraft -- yes --> Resume["dispatch RESUME_ROUND { draft }"]
310	  Resume --> RState["reducer: restore allTiles, bankTileIds,\nzones, activeSlotIndex, phase,\nroundIndex, retryCount, levelIndex"]
311	  RState --> RClear["clear dragActiveTileId,\ndragHoverZoneIndex, dragHoverBankTileId"]
312	
313	  HasDraft -- no --> HasTiles{config.initialTiles\n+ initialZones set?}
314	  HasTiles -- yes --> Init["dispatch INIT_ROUND { tiles, zones }"]
315	  HasTiles -- no --> Noop([no-op: state already makeInitialState])
316	  Init --> IState["reducer: populate round state,\nreset roundIndex/retryCount/levelIndex to 0"]
317	
318	  RClear --> Sync
319	  IState --> Sync
320	  Sync["useAnswerGameDraftSync\n(debounce 500 ms)"] --> DB[("session_history_index.draftState")]
321	```
322	
323	### When each action fires
324	
325	| Situation                                                         | Dispatched action | Reducer behavior                                                                                  |
326	| ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
327	| First visit to a game (no persisted session)                      | `INIT_ROUND`      | Populate tiles/zones from config; reset progress counters to 0                                    |
328	| Reload mid-game with an aligned draft                             | `RESUME_ROUND`    | Restore the draft snapshot wholesale; keep `config`; clear drag state                             |
329	| Reload with a stale draft (WordSpell safety net drops the draft)  | `INIT_ROUND`      | `WordSpell.tsx` sets `initialState={undefined}` when `staleDraft` is true; provider sees no draft |
330	| Mid-mount `initialState` change (e.g. draft written by a sibling) | `RESUME_ROUND`    | Effect re-runs; draft flows into reducer without remounting                                       |
331	| Mid-mount `config.initialTiles` change (no draft)                 | `INIT_ROUND`      | Effect re-runs; new tiles populate the round                                                      |
332	
333	The split exists so `INIT_ROUND`'s original "reset progress to 0" semantics
334	stay intact while resumed sessions get a dedicated action that cannot
335	accidentally drop `roundIndex`, `retryCount`, or `levelIndex`.
336
```
</details></tool-use>

1	import {
2	  draggable,
3	  dropTargetForElements,
4	} from '@atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop/element/adapter';
5	import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react';
6	import { flashBankTileRejectFeedback } from './bank-tile-reject-feedback';
7	import { useAnswerGameContext } from './useAnswerGameContext';
8	import { useAnswerGameDispatch } from './useAnswerGameDispatch';
9	import { useAutoNextSlot } from './useAutoNextSlot';
10	import { useGameTTS } from './useGameTTS';
11	import { useTileEvaluation } from './useTileEvaluation';
12	import { useTouchDrag } from './useTouchDrag';
13	import type { TileItem } from './types';
14	import type { TouchDragHandlers } from './useTouchDrag';
15	import type { RefObject } from 'react';
16	import { useSettings } from '@/db/hooks/useSettings';
17	import { playSound } from '@/lib/audio/AudioFeedback';
18	import { getGameEventBus } from '@/lib/game-event-bus';
19	import { resolveSkin } from '@/lib/skin';
20	
21	export interface DraggableTile extends TouchDragHandlers {
22	  ref: RefObject<HTMLButtonElement | null>;
23	  handleClick: () => void;
24	}
25	
26	export const useDraggableTile = (tile: TileItem): DraggableTile => {
27	  const ref = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null);
28	  const dispatch = useAnswerGameDispatch();
29	  const state = useAnswerGameContext();
30	  // Mirror state in a ref so onDragStart can read current gameId/roundIndex
31	  // without adding `state` to the draggable() effect's deps (which would
32	  // re-subscribe pragmatic-drag-and-drop on every game-state change).
33	  const stateRef = useRef(state);
34	  const { placeInNextSlot } = useAutoNextSlot();
35	  const { speakTile } = useGameTTS();
36	  const speakTileRef = useRef(speakTile);
37	  const { placeTile } = useTileEvaluation();
38	  const { settings } = useSettings();
39	  const skin = resolveSkin(state.config.gameId, state.config.skin);
40	
41	  useEffect(() => {
42	    speakTileRef.current = speakTile;
43	  }, [speakTile]);
44	
45	  useEffect(() => {
46	    stateRef.current = state;
47	  }, [state]);
48	
49	  // HTML5 DnD — desktop
50	  // SET_DRAG_ACTIVE on start so slots can show a preview while the bank tile
51	  // is being dragged. Cleared on drop (slot handler also clears it on success,
52	  // so the double-clear here is idempotent).
53	  // Also registered as a drop target so slot tiles can be dropped onto this
54	  // bank tile to trigger SWAP_SLOT_BANK. onDragEnter sets the hover preview;
55	  // hover is cleared by SET_DRAG_ACTIVE(null) when the drag ends.
56	  useEffect(() => {
57	    const element = ref.current;
58	    if (!element) return;
59	    const cleanupDraggable = draggable({
60	      element,
61	      getInitialData: () => ({ tileId: tile.id }),
62	      onDragStart: () => {
63	        speakTileRef.current(tile.label);
64	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: tile.id });
65	        getGameEventBus().emit({
66	          type: 'game:drag-start',
67	          gameId: stateRef.current.config.gameId,
68	          sessionId: '',
69	          profileId: '',
70	          timestamp: Date.now(),
71	          roundIndex: stateRef.current.roundIndex,
72	          tileId: tile.id,
73	        });
74	      },
75	      onDrop: () => dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null }),
76	    });
77	    const cleanupDropTarget = dropTargetForElements({
78	      element,
79	      getData: () => ({ bankTileId: tile.id, isBankTarget: true }),
80	      onDragEnter: () =>
81	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER_BANK', tileId: tile.id }),
82	      onDragLeave: () =>
83	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER_BANK', tileId: null }),
84	    });
85	    return () => {
86	      cleanupDraggable();
87	      cleanupDropTarget();
88	    };
89	  }, [tile.id, tile.label, dispatch]);
90	
91	  // Pointer-events drag — touch / mobile
92	  const { onPointerDown, onPointerMove, onPointerUp, onPointerCancel } =
93	    useTouchDrag({
94	      tileId: tile.id,
95	      label: tile.label,
96	      onDragStart: () => {
97	        speakTileRef.current(tile.label);
98	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: tile.id });
99	        getGameEventBus().emit({
100	          type: 'game:drag-start',
101	          gameId: stateRef.current.config.gameId,
102	          sessionId: '',
103	          profileId: '',
104	          timestamp: Date.now(),
105	          roundIndex: stateRef.current.roundIndex,
106	          tileId: tile.id,
107	        });
108	      },
109	      onDragCancel: () =>
110	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null }),
111	      onDrop: (droppedTileId, zoneIndex) => {
112	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null });
113	        const { correct } = placeTile(droppedTileId, zoneIndex);
114	        if (
115	          !correct &&
116	          stateRef.current.config.wrongTileBehavior === 'reject'
117	        ) {
118	          requestAnimationFrame(() => {
119	            flashBankTileRejectFeedback(droppedTileId, {
120	              element: ref.current,
121	            });
122	          });
123	        }
124	      },
125	      onDropOnBank: () =>
126	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null }),
127	      onDropOnBankTile: () =>
128	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null }),
129	      onHoverZone: (zoneIndex) => {
130	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER', zoneIndex });
131	        if (zoneIndex !== null) {
132	          getGameEventBus().emit({
133	            type: 'game:drag-over-zone',
134	            gameId: stateRef.current.config.gameId,
135	            sessionId: '',
136	            profileId: '',
137	            timestamp: Date.now(),
138	            roundIndex: stateRef.current.roundIndex,
139	            zoneIndex,
140	          });
141	        }
142	      },
143	      tapForgivenessThreshold: settings.tapForgivenessThreshold,
144	      onTapFallback: () => {
145	        dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null });
146	        placeInNextSlot(tile.id);
147	      },
148	    });
149	
150	  const handleClick = () => {
151	    if (ref.current?.dataset.shaking) return;
152	
153	    speakTile(tile.label);
154	    const result = placeInNextSlot(tile.id);
155	
156	    const bankEl = ref.current;
157	    if (result.rejected && bankEl) {
158	      bankEl.dataset.shaking = 'true';
159	      flashBankTileRejectFeedback(tile.id, {
160	        element: bankEl,
161	        onAnimationEnd: () => {
162	          delete bankEl.dataset.shaking;
163	        },
164	      });
165	
166	      if (!skin.suppressDefaultSounds) {
167	        playSound('wrong', 0.8);
168	      }
169	
170	      // Tap/click path only — drag rejects are handled in useTileEvaluation.placeTile
171	      dispatch({
172	        type: 'REJECT_TAP',
173	        tileId: tile.id,
174	        zoneIndex: result.zoneIndex,
175	      });
176	
177	      getGameEventBus().emit({
178	        type: 'game:evaluate',
179	        gameId: state.config.gameId,
180	        sessionId: '',
181	        profileId: '',
182	        timestamp: Date.now(),
183	        roundIndex: state.roundIndex,
184	        answer: tile.id,
185	        correct: false,
186	        nearMiss: false,
187	        zoneIndex: result.zoneIndex,
188	        expected: state.zones[result.zoneIndex]?.expectedValue ?? '',
189	      });
190	    }
191	  };
192	
193	  return {
194	    ref,
195	    handleClick,
196	    onPointerDown,
197	    onPointerMove,
198	    onPointerUp,
199	    onPointerCancel,
200	  };
201	};
202
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-02 03:29:21Z)**_

Now I have a clear picture of the bug. When the tap forgiveness check detects a zone under the pointer (line 319), it falls through to the normal drop path but with the ghost already removed and `ghostRef.current` set to null. This means:
- `ghostHalfW` = 0, `ghostHalfH` = 0
- All corner points degenerate to the center point

This means priority items 3/4/5/6 (corner-based expanded detection) lose their expanded hit area. However, since we've already confirmed there IS a zone under the center point (that's why `zoneUnderPointer` was true and we fell through), priority 1 ("Center -> zone") will catch it. So the drop still works correctly -- the zone was detected at the center, the center check in the normal path will also find it. The corner expansion is lost, but it's irrelevant because the center already succeeded.

Wait -- let me re-read more carefully. The tap forgiveness zone check uses ONLY `e.clientX, e.clientY` (center). If a zone is found there, it falls through. Then the normal path also checks center first (priority 1). So the drop will be successful via the center check.

But what about the case where the zone is under a ghost corner but NOT at the center? In that scenario, the tap forgiveness code would find no zone at center, so `zoneUnderPointer` would be false, and it would fire `onTapFallback` -- potentially missing a valid zone drop that would have been detected by the corner expansion in the normal path.

Let me think about whether this matters. For this to be an issue:
1. `dist < tapForgivenessThreshold` (the drag is small, < 20px)  
2. No zone at center point
3. BUT a zone would be at a corner point

Given that the drag is so small (< 20px), and the ghost is typically 60x60px, the ghost corners extend 30px from center. So it's plausible that a zone is under a corner but not under the center. In that case, tap forgiveness fires instead of the drop -- but this is actually the DESIRED behavior. The whole point is: if the user barely moved, treat it as a tap, not a drag. The zone-check is just a safety valve so that if the user is clearly over a zone, respect the drop. Checking only center makes sense for this "are they clearly over a zone?" heuristic.

Now let me check the `cleanup()` at the end of `onPointerUp` (line 466). After the tap forgiveness path already called `cleanup()` and returned, the final `cleanup()` is only reached in the non-tap-forgiveness paths.

Let me verify one more edge case: what happens when `tapForgivenessThreshold` is passed but `onTapFallback` is undefined (the slot tile case mentioned in the diff).

Looking at line 299-301:
```typescript
if (
  tapForgivenessThreshold !== undefined &&
  onTapFallbackRef.current
) {
```

The slot tile in `useSlotTileDrag.ts` does NOT pass `tapForgivenessThreshold`, so it would be `undefined`. The check would short-circuit. Good.

But wait -- what if someone passes `tapForgivenessThreshold` but NOT `onTapFallback`? The condition requires both. The code correctly skips the tap forgiveness block. No bug there.

Now let me check a subtle issue: the `tapForgivenessThreshold` is in the `useCallback` deps array but `onTapFallback` is NOT (it's accessed via ref). This is correct -- the ref pattern avoids recreating the callback when the function changes.

Let me also check whether `startPos.current` could be null when accessed in the tap forgiveness block. At line 294, there's already a guard `if (e.pointerType === 'mouse' || !startPos.current) return;`. So by line 303, `startPos.current` is guaranteed non-null. Good.

Let me now look at the `!isDragging.current` case in `onPointerUp`. What if the user does a very quick touch where the pointer is released without any move (no drag threshold crossed)?

1	# Agent instructions (BaseSkill)
2	
3	This file is for **any** coding agent (Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, etc.). Prefer it as the first stop for **process**; implementation detail lives in [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md).
4	
5	## Git and worktrees (mandatory)
6	
7	- Do **not** edit the codebase from the main repo checkout unless the user explicitly asks to work in the root tree (e.g. “skip worktree”, “patch main directly”).
8	- **Before the first file change in a session**, confirm the workspace path is under `worktrees/<task-name>/`. If it is not, create a worktree from `origin/master` and continue there:
9	
10	  ```bash
11	  git fetch origin master
12	  git worktree add ./worktrees/<task-name> -b feat/<task-name> origin/master
13	  cd ./worktrees/<task-name>
14	  yarn install
15	  ```
16	
17	- Never commit to `master`. Branch → PR → merge.
18	- **Full paths in output:** When referencing files in messages, always print the **full path including the worktree directory** (e.g. `worktrees/my-branch/docs/plans/foo.md`), not just the repo-relative path. Relative paths are ambiguous across worktrees.
19	- Project rules also live in `.cursor/rules/` (see `git-workflow.mdc`); this file and those rules should agree.
20	
21	## Before writing a plan
22	
23	Plans are binding source of truth for executor agents. If a plan will create or modify any of the following, **invoke the corresponding project skill before prescribing code**, and let the skill override plan boilerplate:
24	
25	| Plan touches                                 | Required skill                                       |
26	| -------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
27	| `*.stories.tsx`                              | `write-storybook`                                    |
28	| `tests-vr/**`, `tests-e2e/**`, `*.e2e.ts`    | `write-e2e-vr-tests`                                 |
29	| `*.md` authored by an agent                  | Markdown Authoring rules in [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md) |
30	| Architecture docs (`*.mdx` under game state) | `update-architecture-docs`                           |
31	
32	If a plan's code sample conflicts with a skill, flag it as a **Spec Delta** at the top of the plan and justify the deviation — don't silently ship boilerplate that contradicts project convention. Executors must load the corresponding skill into context when the plan prescribes matching files; if the plan and skill disagree and there is no Spec Delta, treat the skill as authoritative and flag the conflict before coding.
33	
34	See [CLAUDE.md → Before writing a plan](./CLAUDE.md#before-writing-a-plan) for the canonical version.
35	
36	## Commits (baby steps)
37	
38	- After each **coherent** chunk of work (feature slice, bugfix + test, doc section, rule tweak), **commit** with a clear message. Do not wait until the end of a large task to make one giant commit.
39	- **Multiple commits in one PR are expected and preferred.** Reviewers can follow the story; you or the user can **cherry-pick** or **revert** a single commit without unpicking everything.
40	- If the user asks for “baby step” commits, treat that as the default for the rest of the branch.
41	
42	## Where to look next
43	
44	- [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md) — React/ESLint expectations, TDD, markdown, CI buckets, VR tests.
45	- `.cursor/rules/` — Cursor-specific always-on rules.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-02 03:29:33Z)**_

If `isDragging.current` is false (user lifted without crossing the 8px drag threshold), the entire `if (isDragging.current)` block is skipped, and we go straight to `cleanup()` at line 466. No tap forgiveness fires, no onDrop fires, nothing happens. This means a "pure tap" (zero displacement) goes through the normal click handler path, which is correct -- the touch drag hook doesn't interfere, and the button's onClick fires instead.

Let me check the test for the boundary case at the threshold value. The test at line 172-198 asserts that exactly 20px does NOT fire tap fallback. This is correct per the `dist < tapForgivenessThreshold` (strict less-than). Good.

Now let me look at one more potential issue: `onTapFallback` in `useDraggableTile.ts` calls `placeInNextSlot(tile.id)` but does NOT call `speakTile`. The `handleClick` path calls both `speakTile` AND `placeInNextSlot`. Is this a missing behavior? Let me check if the drag start already spoke the tile.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-02 03:29:37Z)**_

Let me carefully trace the boundary test logic before writing the findings.

The boundary test (test 5) moves to clientX=109 in onPointerMove (9px from start=100), which is just above `DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX = 8`, so `isDragging` becomes `true`. Then onPointerUp is at clientX=120. Distance from startPos (100,100) to (120,100) is exactly 20px. The comparison is `dist < tapForgivenessThreshold` (i.e., `20 < 20`), which is `false`, so `onTapFallback` is NOT called. The test name says "exactly at threshold does NOT fire" and asserts `expect(onTapFallback).not.toHaveBeenCalled()`. This is consistent with the implementation.

However: in test 1 (happy path), the onPointerMove is at clientX=110 (10px from start=100), which exceeds `DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX=8`, so `isDragging.current` becomes `true`. Then onPointerUp at clientX=112: displacement from start (100,100) to (112,100) is 12px, which IS less than threshold 20. So `onTapFallback` fires. This is correct.

Test 2 (exceeds threshold): move to 125 (25px > 8), isDragging=true. Up at 130: dist=30 > 20, so no tap fallback. Correct.

Now let me check the critical gap: what happens if `onPointerUp` fires but `isDragging` is `false`? The entire tap forgiveness block is inside `if (isDragging.current)`. So a true "tap" (pointer down then immediately up, no move that exceeds DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX=8) will NOT trigger `onTapFallback` — it falls through to `cleanup()`. None of the tests cover this case. This is a real untested branch: a user taps so quickly that `isDragging` never becomes true.

Also: the test for the "slot tile case" (test 4) omits `onTapFallback` but keeps `tapForgivenessThreshold: 20`. The implementation gate is `tapForgivenessThreshold !== undefined && onTapFallbackRef.current`. With threshold=20 but no callback, the outer `if` evaluates `onTapFallbackRef.current` as falsy, so execution falls through to normal drop detection, then eventually hits `onDragCancelRef.current?.()` since no zone is found. The test asserts `onDragCancel` was called. This is correct behavior.

Let me also check whether `useSettings` receives tests for the new `tapForgivenessThreshold` default.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-02 03:29:43Z)**_

The `onDragStart` already calls `speakTileRef.current(tile.label)`. So for the tap forgiveness flow:
1. User touches down
2. User moves slightly (> 8px drag threshold) -- `onDragStart` fires, speaking the tile
3. User lifts (< 20px from start) -- `onTapFallback` fires, placing tile without re-speaking

This makes sense. The tile was already spoken when the drag started, so re-speaking on tap fallback would be redundant.

Now let me check whether `onTapFallback` is missing the reject-feedback path that `handleClick` has. In `handleClick`, if `placeInNextSlot` returns `rejected: true`, it does shake animation and sound. In `onTapFallback`, it just calls `placeInNextSlot` without checking the result. This means:
- A rejected tap-forgiveness placement won't shake or play the wrong sound
- It won't dispatch `REJECT_TAP`
- It won't emit `game:evaluate`

This could be intentional (simpler path, drag already showed feedback) or could be a missed behavior. Let me check if `placeInNextSlot` itself handles the reject case.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-02 03:29:58Z)**_

Now I have a complete picture. Let me analyse the findings and write the output.

Key observations:

1. **`tapForgivenessThreshold` in `useCallback` deps but `onTapFallback` is not** — `onPointerUp` closes over `tapForgivenessThreshold` (a primitive, fine) and reads `onTapFallbackRef.current` (correct ref pattern). But `onTapFallback` itself is omitted from the `useCallback` dep array. Since the ref pattern handles the staleness correctly, this is fine by design. No issue here.

2. **Coupled feature flags** — `tapForgivenessThreshold !== undefined && onTapFallbackRef.current` — these two checks are independently optional, meaning silence of one while the other is present silently no-ops. The JSDoc says they pair, but TypeScript doesn't enforce it. This is a soft design smell.

3. **Ghost removal before `cleanup()` in tap path** — the tap forgiveness branch manually does `ghostRef.current?.el.remove(); ghostRef.current = null;` and then calls `cleanup()`, which calls `cleanupGhost()`. `cleanupGhost` checks `cleanupInProgressRef` but still tries `ghostRef.current?.el.remove()` (safe, already null). The double-cleanup is benign but slightly confusing.

4. **`onTapFallback` missing from the `useCallback` dep array for `onPointerUp`** — wait, let me re-read. The dep array is `[tileId, onDrop, onDropOnBank, onDropOnBankTile, tapForgivenessThreshold, cleanup]`. `onTapFallback` is not there. However `onTapFallbackRef.current` is used inside, not `onTapFallback` directly — the ref pattern is the standard workaround. This is correct, not a bug.

5. **Slider `onValueChange` destructuring `[v]` with no type annotation** — in settings.tsx line 142 `([v]) =>` — `v` is inferred as `number` from the Slider prop signature. No issue.

6. **`settings.tapForgivenessThreshold` is `number | undefined` at the TypeScript level** — `useSettings` returns `typeof DEFAULT_SETTINGS & Partial<SettingsDoc>`. Since `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` includes `tapForgivenessThreshold: 20`, the intersection means the field is `number` (not optional) at the type level. So no `?? 20` is strictly needed in settings.tsx. However, the `?? 20` is defensive and harmless.

7. **`onTapFallback` called without clearing hover state** — in the tap forgiveness path, `onTapFallbackRef.current()` is called, then `cleanup()`. But `lastHoverZoneRef` and `lastHoverBankTileRef` are only cleared inside `cleanupGhost()`, which `cleanup()` does call. This is fine.

8. **The `onPointerDown` early exit guard `!tileId`** — pre-existing, not part of this PR.

9. **`onTapFallback` in `useDraggableTile` calls `placeInNextSlot(tile.id)` without checking result** — the `handleClick` path checks `result.rejected` and dispatches `REJECT_TAP`, plays a sound, etc. The `onTapFallback` path silently ignores rejections. This is a behavioural regression risk: a tap-forgiveness placement gets no rejection feedback.

10. **`plainSettingsFromQuery` cast `doc as { toJSON: () => SettingsDoc }` is an unchecked cast** — pre-existing.

11. **Schema version not bumped** — `settings.ts` schema `version: 0` is unchanged after adding a new field. RxDB requires a version bump when a schema changes. This is a genuine bug.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-02 03:30:04Z)**_

So `placeInNextSlot` can return `{ placed: false, rejected: true }` without placing the tile. In the `onTapFallback` handler at line 144-147 of `useDraggableTile.ts`:
```typescript
onTapFallback: () => {
  dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null });
  placeInNextSlot(tile.id);
},
```

The return value is discarded. If the tile is rejected, the user gets no visual/audio feedback. The `handleClick` path (line 150-190) does handle this case with shake + sound. This is arguably a behavioral inconsistency but not a correctness bug -- the placement is still correctly rejected (tile stays in bank), just without the reject animation/sound.

This is more of a UX/design concern than a correctness bug, so I'll note it as a lower-severity finding since the tile rejection logic itself is correct.

Let me also check the `useCallback` dependency array for `onPointerUp` to see if `tapForgivenessThreshold` changing would cause stale closure issues.

    },

      "owner": "maintainability",
      "requires_verification": false,
      "pre_existing": false,
      "suggested_fix": "Add an inline comment explaining why the tap-forgiveness zone check intentionally uses only the center point (no ghost corners), as opposed to the expanded multi-corner sampling done in the normal drop path. Without this, a future maintainer will read the two parallel `elementsFromPoint` loops and assume the single-point version is a bug and 'fix' it, silently changing tap-vs-drop semantics."
    },
    {

      "severity": "P2",

      "line": 144,
      "confidence": 75,

      "pre_existing": false,
      "suggested_fix": "Extract the shared core of handleClick (speakTile + placeInNextSlot + rejection side-effects) into an internal `handleTap()` function used by both handleClick and onTapFallback. Currently onTapFallback only dispatches SET_DRAG_ACTIVE(null) and calls placeInNextSlot — it skips speakTile, the rejection flash, the REJECT_TAP dispatch, and the game:evaluate event. These omissions may be intentional but are undocumented and will silently diverge from handleClick as that path evolves."
    }
  ],

      "title": "settings schema version was not bumped after adding tapForgivenessThreshold field",
      "description": "The field is optional and has a default in DEFAULT_SETTINGS, so existing persisted documents will not break on read. However, RxDB's version-0 collection has no migration strategy registered; if a future schema change requires bumping the version, the author will need to retroactively assign a strategy for this field addition, which will be confusing since version 0 is the baseline. This is a latent confusion risk rather than a runtime bug.",
      "severity": "P3"
    },
    {

      "description": "DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX = 8 is the point at which a drag starts. The schema minimum of 8 prevents setting the forgiveness threshold below the drag threshold, which is correct — but the connection is undocumented. If DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX is ever changed, the schema minimum will silently become wrong.",
      "severity": "P3"

After the ghost is eagerly nulled at line 308-309, the normal drop path at line 332 reads ghostRef.current?.halfW ?? 0, which will be 0 — meaning ghost-corner expansion uses zero-size corners. A test that asserts onDrop is called with the correct zone index in this scenario would catch regressions here."
    },

      "title": "Ghost removed before zone-present fallthrough loses corner-based drop expansion",
      "severity": "P3",
      "file": "/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/298-tap-forgiveness/src/components/answer-game/useTouchDrag.ts",
      "line": 308,
      "confidence": 75,
      "autofix_class": "advisory",
      "owner": "downstream-resolver",
      "requires_verification": false,
      "pre_existing": false,
      "suggested_fix": "When the tap-forgiveness zone check finds a zone at center (zoneUnderPointer=true) and falls through to the normal drop path, ghostRef.current is already null so ghostHalfW/ghostHalfH resolve to 0. All corner points degenerate to center. This is harmless because center already passed the zone check (priority 1 will succeed), but if a future refactor adds logic between the tap-forgiveness check and center-zone check, this invariant could break. Consider saving halfW/halfH before removing the ghost, or only removing the ghost in the !zoneUnderPointer branch."
    },
    {
      "title": "onTapFallback discards placeInNextSlot result — no reject feedback on micro-drag placement",
      "severity": "P3",
      "file": "/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/298-tap-forgiveness/src/components/answer-game/useDraggableTile.ts",
      "line": 144,
      "confidence": 75,
      "autofix_class": "manual",
      "owner": "downstream-resolver",
      "requires_verification": true,
      "pre_existing": false,
      "suggested_fix": "The onTapFallback handler calls placeInNextSlot(tile.id) but ignores the return value. If the tile is rejected (wrong tile in 'reject' mode), the user sees no shake animation, no wrong sound, no REJECT_TAP dispatch, and no game:evaluate event — unlike the equivalent handleClick path (lines 157-189). Either mirror the reject handling from handleClick, or document this as intentional (drag-start already provided audio feedback via speakTile)."
    }
  ],
  "residual_risks": [
    "Tap forgiveness zone check samples only center point. A micro-drag that ends with the pointer just outside a zone but whose ghost corner would overlap the zone triggers onTapFallback instead of a drop. This is arguably correct for the 'forgive micro-drags' intent, but differs from the normal path's expanded corner detection.",
    "tapForgivenessThreshold is stored in RxDB per profile. If the DB read is slow or returns undefined during initial render, the default (20) from useSettings covers it, but a momentary flash where threshold is undefined would disable tap forgiveness until the DB doc loads."
  ],
  "testing_gaps": [
    "No test for the fallthrough case where tap-forgiveness detects a zone under the pointer and still completes the drop via the normal path (verifying the ghost-removed state doesn't break zone detection).",
    "No test for the rejected-tile scenario through onTapFallback — if a wrong tile triggers tap forgiveness, there's no verification of what feedback (if any) the user gets.",
    "No integration test verifying that changing tapForgivenessThreshold in settings propagates to an active game session without requiring a page reload."
  ]
}
ENDJSON

    "tapForgivenessThreshold is stored in RxDB per profile. If the DB read is slow or returns undefined during initial render, the default (20) from useSettings covers it, but a momentary flash where threshold is undefined would disable tap forgiveness until the DB doc loads."

1:# Touch Keyboard for Type Mode — Implementation Plan
5:**Goal:** Fix type mode on touch devices by focusing a hidden native `<input>` when a slot is tapped, triggering the device keyboard, and capturing typed characters via the `input` event.
7:**Architecture:** A `TouchKeyboardContext` provides `focusKeyboard` to slot components. `AnswerGameProvider` conditionally renders either a `TouchKeyboardAdapter` (touch) or the existing `KeyboardInputAdapter` (desktop). Slot components are consolidated into a shared `OrderedSlots` component that handles both the tap-to-focus and scroll-into-view behaviours once.
9:**Tech Stack:** React, TypeScript, Vitest + @testing-library/react, TailwindCSS, @atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/spec-swap-slot-bank.md`

```text
1	# Spec: Swap Slot ↔ Bank Tile on Drag
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	When a user drags a tile from a slot and drops it onto a **visible bank tile** (not an
6	empty hole), the two tiles swap positions unconditionally — no evaluation, no sound,
7	no wrong-state. The slot tile goes to the bank; the bank tile goes to the slot.
8	
9	Dropping on an **empty bank hole** (tile already placed in another slot) continues to
10	behave as today: `REMOVE_TILE` — the slot tile returns to the bank.
11	
12	## New Reducer Action
13	
14	```ts
15	| { type: 'SWAP_SLOT_BANK'; zoneIndex: number; bankTileId: string }
16	```
17	
18	### Logic
19	
20	```
21	guard: zones[zoneIndex].placedTileId !== null
22	guard: bankTileIds.includes(bankTileId)
23	
24	slotTileId = zones[zoneIndex].placedTileId
25	
26	newBankTileIds = bankTileIds
27	  .filter(id => id !== bankTileId)
28	  .concat(slotTileId)
29	
30	newZones[zoneIndex] = {
31	  ...zone,
32	  placedTileId: bankTileId,
33	  isWrong:      false,
34	  isLocked:     false,
35	}
36	
37	return { ...state, bankTileIds: newBankTileIds, zones: newZones, dragActiveTileId: null }
38	```
39	
40	No `retryCount` change, no phase transition.
41	
42	## Desktop HTML5 DnD
43	
44	Register each bank tile button as a `dropTargetForElements` target (inside
45	`useDraggableTile`):
46	
47	```ts
48	dropTargetForElements({
49	  element,
50	  getData: () => ({ bankTileId: tile.id, isBankTarget: true }),
51	});
52	```
53	
54	In `useSlotTileDrag.onDrop`, check `targets[0]?.data['bankTileId']` before the
55	existing `isBankTarget` check:
56	
57	```ts
58	const bankTileId = targets[0]?.data['bankTileId'];
59	if (typeof bankTileId === 'string') {
60	  dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null });
61	  dispatch({ type: 'SWAP_SLOT_BANK', zoneIndex, bankTileId });
62	  return;
63	}
64	// existing isBankTarget → REMOVE_TILE path
65	```
66	
67	When the slot tile is dropped on an **empty hole**, no bank-tile drop target fires
68	(empty holes are plain divs with no `dropTargetForElements`), so `targets[0]` is the
69	bank container (`isBankTarget: true` only) → falls through to `REMOVE_TILE` ✓.
70	
71	## Touch
72	
73	### Detection
74	
75	`data-tile-bank-hole={tile.id}` is already on every bank hole div (both visible and
76	empty). Add a helper to `useTouchDrag` that scans `elementsFromPoint` for this
77	attribute:
78	
79	```ts
80	const findBankTileAt = (px, py): string | null => {
81	  for (const el of document.elementsFromPoint(px, py)) {
82	    if (el instanceof HTMLElement) {
83	      const holeId = el.dataset['tileBankHole'];
84	      if (holeId) return holeId;
85	    }
86	  }
87	  return null;
88	};
89	```
90	
91	### New callback
92	
93	Add to `UseTouchDragOptions`:
94	
95	```ts
96	/** Called when the ghost is released over a specific bank tile's hole. */
97	onDropOnBankTile?: (bankTileId: string) => void;
98	```
99	
100	### Priority in `onPointerUp`
101	
102	Replace the bank-hit branches with:
103	
104	```
105	if (bankTileId found at that point && onDropOnBankTile provided)
106	  → onDropOnBankTile(bankTileId)
107	else if (isBankAt)
108	  → onDropOnBank()
109	```
110	
111	Apply this for both the **center** check (step 2) and the **corner** check (step 4).
112	
113	### Caller wiring (`useSlotTileDrag`)
114	
115	```ts
116	const handleTouchDropOnBankTile = useCallback(
117	  (bankTileId: string) => {
118	    dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null });
119	    if (bankTileIds.includes(bankTileId)) {
120	      dispatch({ type: 'SWAP_SLOT_BANK', zoneIndex, bankTileId });
121	    } else {
122	      // Dropped on empty hole → return to bank
123	      dispatch({ type: 'REMOVE_TILE', zoneIndex });
124	    }
125	  },
126	  [dispatch, zoneIndex, bankTileIds],
127	);
128	```
129	
130	`useSlotTileDrag` needs to pull `bankTileIds` from `useAnswerGameContext`.
131	
132	## Files to Change
133	
134	| File                     | Change                                                                                                        |
135	| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
136	| `types.ts`               | Add `SWAP_SLOT_BANK` action                                                                                   |
137	| `answer-game-reducer.ts` | Add `SWAP_SLOT_BANK` case                                                                                     |
138	| `useDraggableTile.ts`    | Register `dropTargetForElements` on bank tile button                                                          |
139	| `useSlotTileDrag.ts`     | Handle `bankTileId` in HTML5 `onDrop`; add touch `onDropOnBankTile` handler; import context for `bankTileIds` |
140	| `useTouchDrag.ts`        | Add `onDropOnBankTile` option + `findBankTileAt` helper; update bank-hit branches                             |
141	
142	## Out of Scope
143	
144	- No hover preview for the bank-tile swap target (could be added later).
145	- No sound or animation specific to this swap.
146	- No evaluation — the tile lands in the slot as-is (`isWrong: false, isLocked: false`).
147
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-02 03:31:35Z)**_

Now I have all the information needed. Let me write the final analysis.

Key findings to document:

1. **P2 - Boundary test uses wrong displacement axis for the "isDragging activation" move**: In test 5 (boundary), the `onPointerMove` goes to clientX=109 (9px from start=100), which is just over `DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX=8`, making `isDragging=true`. The up event is at clientX=120 (20px from start). But the displacement measured in `onPointerUp` is from `startPos` (set by `onPointerDown`) to `e.clientX/Y` of the up event — that is `(120-100)=20px`. The test is correct in intent and aligns with the implementation. However, the comment says "Exactly 20px from start" which is accurate.

2. **P2 - No test for `tapForgivenessThreshold=undefined` with `onTapFallback` defined**: The implementation guard is a two-part `&&`. There's a test when `onTapFallback` is undefined but `threshold` is set. There's no test for the inverse: `tapForgivenessThreshold=undefined` but `onTapFallback` is provided. This is a valid untested branch with behavioral consequence.

3. **P2 - No test for onPointerUp when `isDragging=false`**: The entire forgiveness block is inside `if (isDragging.current)`. A true micro-tap (pointer down + immediately up, never crossing DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX=8) never reaches the forgiveness code. `onTapFallback` will never fire in this scenario. This is a real user scenario (ultra-fast tap) that's untested.

4. **P3 - `useSettings` test file has no test for `tapForgivenessThreshold` default**: The new `tapForgivenessThreshold: 20` in `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` is behavioral code but the `useSettings.test.tsx` doesn't test it. This is a weaker gap since the default is simple and the hook test pattern uses real DB.

5. **P3 - `settings.tsx` slider UI has no test at all**: The new slider and `onValueChange` are new behavioral code with no corresponding test. (But there was no pre-existing settings.tsx test to begin with, and route tests don't exist for this file.)

102	async function addBaseSkillCollections(
103	  db: RxDatabase<BaseSkillCollections>,
104	): Promise<BaseSkillDatabase> {
105	  await db.addCollections(COLLECTIONS);
106	  return db as BaseSkillDatabase;
107	}

109	/** Memory storage for unit tests; does not run migrations (call `checkVersionAndMigrate` in tests that need `app_meta`). */
110	export async function createTestDatabase(): Promise<BaseSkillDatabase> {
111	  const db = await createRxDatabase<BaseSkillDatabase>({
112	    name: `${DB_NAME}-${nanoid()}`,
113	    storage: wrappedValidateAjvStorage({
114	      storage: getRxStorageMemory(),
115	    }),
116	    multiInstance: false,
117	  });
118	  return addBaseSkillCollections(db);
119	}

121	const STORYBOOK_DB_PREFIX = 'baseskill-storybook';
122	
123	/**
124	 * Ephemeral in-memory DB for Storybook. Browsers do not allow replacing native
125	 * `indexedDB` with fake-indexeddb, so demos must use memory storage instead.
126	 */
127	export async function createStorybookDatabase(): Promise<BaseSkillDatabase> {
128	  const db = await createRxDatabase<BaseSkillDatabase>({
129	    name: `${STORYBOOK_DB_PREFIX}-${nanoid()}`,
130	    storage: getRxStorageMemory(),
131	    multiInstance: false,
132	  });
133	  const withCols = await addBaseSkillCollections(db);
134	  await checkVersionAndMigrate(withCols);
135	  return withCols;
136	}
137	
138	let productionDbPromise: Promise<BaseSkillDatabase> | undefined;
139	
140	/** Browser-only: opens IndexedDB via Dexie-backed RxStorage and runs migrations. */
141	export async function getOrCreateDatabase(): Promise<BaseSkillDatabase> {
142	  if (typeof indexedDB === 'undefined') {
143	    throw new TypeError(
144	      'getOrCreateDatabase is only available in a browser with IndexedDB',
145	    );
146	  }
147	  productionDbPromise ??= (async () => {
148	    const open = async (): Promise<BaseSkillDatabase> => {
149	      const db = await createRxDatabase<BaseSkillDatabase>({
150	        name: PRODUCTION_DB_NAME,
151	        storage: getRxStorageDexie(),
152	        multiInstance: false,
153	      });
154	      try {
155	        const withCols = await addBaseSkillCollections(db);
156	        await checkVersionAndMigrate(withCols);
157	        return withCols;
158	      } catch (error) {
159	        await db.close().catch(() => {});
160	        throw error;
161	      }
162	    };
163	    // RxDB's parallel migration plumbing intermittently throws DM4 with a
164	    // closed-instance inner error during `addCollections` for users whose
165	    // IndexedDB carries stale legacy schema versions. Recover by wiping
166	    // the broken collection's old storage + meta and retrying. Loop a few
167	    // times in case multiple collections trip the same race in one boot.
168	    const MAX_RECOVERY_ATTEMPTS = 5;
169	    for (let attempt = 0; attempt < MAX_RECOVERY_ATTEMPTS; attempt++) {
170	      try {
171	        return await open();
172	      } catch (error) {
173	        const collection = getMigrationFailureCollection(error);
174	        if (!collection) throw error;
175	        await recoverBrokenMigration(collection);
176	      }
177	    }
178	    return await open();
179	  })();
180	  return productionDbPromise;
181	}
182	
183	export async function destroyTestDatabase(
184	  db?: BaseSkillDatabase,
185	): Promise<void> {
186	  if (db) await db.remove();
187	}

      "suggested_fix": "Move the ghost removal and the elementsFromPoint check into a single helper (e.g. `peekZoneWithoutGhost(x, y)`) that (1) hides the ghost, (2) samples the DOM, (3) restores the ghost if the tap path will not fire. The tap path calls this helper; if it returns false, fall through without having mutated ghostRef. This removes the implicit assumption that `ghostRef.current` is null by the time the normal removal block at line 336 runs, and makes the state machine easier to reason about when the tap threshold passes but a zone is found."
    },
    {
      "title": "tapForgivenessThreshold only gates zone detection at pointer center; normal drop path uses multi-corner sampling — inconsistency a future reader will have to decode",
      "severity": "P2",

      "pre_existing": false,
      "suggested_fix": "Add an inline comment explaining why the tap-forgiveness zone check intentionally uses only the center point (no ghost corners), as opposed to the expanded multi-corner sampling done in the normal drop path. Without this, a future maintainer will read the two parallel `elementsFromPoint` loops and assume the single-point version is a bug and 'fix' it, silently changing tap-vs-drop semantics."
    },
    {
      "title": "onTapFallback duplicates the full handleClick body without the rejection/sound/dispatch/emit side-effects, creating two diverging paths for the same user intent",
      "severity": "P2",

      "pre_existing": false,
      "suggested_fix": "Extract the shared core of handleClick (speakTile + placeInNextSlot + rejection side-effects) into an internal `handleTap()` function used by both handleClick and onTapFallback. Currently onTapFallback only dispatches SET_DRAG_ACTIVE(null) and calls placeInNextSlot — it skips speakTile, the rejection flash, the REJECT_TAP dispatch, and the game:evaluate event. These omissions may be intentional but are undocumented and will silently diverge from handleClick as that path evolves."
    }
  ],
  "residual_risks": [
    {
      "title": "settings schema version was not bumped after adding tapForgivenessThreshold field",
      "description": "The field is optional and has a default in DEFAULT_SETTINGS, so existing persisted documents will not break on read. However, RxDB's version-0 collection has no migration strategy registered; if a future schema change requires bumping the version, the author will need to retroactively assign a strategy for this field addition, which will be confusing since version 0 is the baseline. This is a latent confusion risk rather than a runtime bug.",
      "severity": "P3"
    },
    {
      "title": "tapForgivenessThreshold minimum in schema (8) matches DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX constant but the relationship is implicit",
      "description": "DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX = 8 is the point at which a drag starts. The schema minimum of 8 prevents setting the forgiveness threshold below the drag threshold, which is correct — but the connection is undocumented. If DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX is ever changed, the schema minimum will silently become wrong.",
      "severity": "P3"

  "testing_gaps": [

      "title": "No test for the zone-found branch inside the tap forgiveness block (dist &lt; threshold AND zone under pointer): ghost removed but tap NOT fired, fall-through to normal drop",
      "description": "The test at line 110 (useTouchDrag.test.ts) verifies that a zone under the pointer causes onDrop to be called and onTapFallback not to be called, but it does not verify that ghostRef is in a consistent state when this fall-through happens. After the ghost is eagerly nulled at line 308-309, the normal drop path at line 332 reads ghostRef.current?.halfW ?? 0, which will be 0 — meaning ghost-corner expansion uses zero-size corners. A test that asserts onDrop is called with the correct zone index in this scenario would catch regressions here."
    },

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/298-tap-forgiveness/src/db/migrations.ts`

```text
1	import { nanoid } from 'nanoid';
2	import { migrateCustomGames } from './migrate-custom-games';
3	import { MAX_SCHEMA_VERSION } from './schemas';
4	import type { BaseSkillDatabase } from './types';
5	
6	function getAppVersion(): string {
7	  const v: string = import.meta.env.VITE_APP_VERSION;
8	  if (typeof v === 'string' && /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/.test(v)) {
9	    return v;
10	  }
11	  return '0.1.0';
12	}
13	
14	export async function ensureAppMetaSingleton(
15	  db: BaseSkillDatabase,
16	): Promise<void> {
17	  const existing = await db.app_meta.findOne('singleton').exec();
18	  if (existing) {
19	    return;
20	  }
21	  await db.app_meta.insert({
22	    id: 'singleton',
23	    appVersion: getAppVersion(),
24	    rxdbSchemaVersion: MAX_SCHEMA_VERSION,
25	    lastMigrationAt: null,
26	    installId: `dev_${nanoid()}`,
27	  });
28	}
29	
30	export async function checkVersionAndMigrate(
31	  db: BaseSkillDatabase,
32	): Promise<void> {
33	  await ensureAppMetaSingleton(db);
34	  const meta = await db.app_meta.findOne('singleton').exec();
35	  if (!meta) {
36	    return;
37	  }
38	  if (meta.rxdbSchemaVersion < MAX_SCHEMA_VERSION) {
39	    await meta.incrementalPatch({
40	      rxdbSchemaVersion: MAX_SCHEMA_VERSION,
41	      lastMigrationAt: new Date().toISOString(),
42	      appVersion: getAppVersion(),
43	    });
44	  }
45	  await migrateCustomGames(db);
46	}
47
```
</details></tool-use>
