 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-03-drag-drop-game-design.md`

```text
1	# AnswerGame — Shared Primitive Design Spec
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-04-03
4	**Status:** In Review
5	**Milestone:** 5 — Reference Games
6	**Depends on:** M4 (GameEngineProvider, ScoreAnimation, EncouragementAnnouncer, SpeechOutput, event bus)
7	
8	---
9	
10	## 1. Overview
11	
12	`AnswerGame` is a shared React composition primitive that lives at
13	`src/components/answer-game/`. It is **not a game** — it is the
14	infrastructure layer all tile-based answer games in M5 (and beyond) are
15	composed from.
16	
17	Games that use it: WordSpell, NumberMatch, SortNumbers (and any future
18	tile-based game). Each game provides three named slot components
19	(`Question`, `Answer`, `Choices`) and `AnswerGame` handles everything else.
20	
21	**DnD library:** [Pragmatic Drag and Drop](https://atlassian.design/components/pragmatic-drag-and-drop) (shadow effect enabled during drag)
22	
23	---
24	
25	## 2. Responsibilities
26	
27	`AnswerGame` owns — and individual games do **not** re-implement:
28	
29	- Pragmatic DnD context (drag state, pointer capture, shadow effect)
30	- Magnetic snap logic (60 px radius lerp, per [ui-ux.md §8.1](../../ui-ux.md))
31	- Tap-or-drag / auto-next-slot / free-swap interaction modes
32	- Wrong-tile behaviour (`reject` / `lock-auto-eject` / `lock-manual`)
33	- TTS on tile tap (`useGameTTS`)
34	- Drop zone pulse animation while drag is in progress (per [ui-ux.md §8.3](../../ui-ux.md))
35	- Inline confetti burst on round completion (`ScoreAnimation` from M4)
36	- Full-screen game-over celebration (koala mascot + jingle)
37	- Koala mascot encouragement on wrong answer (`EncouragementAnnouncer`)
38	- Session event emission via M4 event bus
39	- Keyboard drag simulation (Space/Enter pick up, Arrow keys move, Escape cancel)
40	
41	---
42	
43	## 3. Compound Component API
44	
45	`AnswerGame` uses the **compound component pattern** — sub-components are
46	attached as static properties and act as layout-position wrappers. Slot
47	implementations are **prop-free**: they read all config via
48	`useAnswerGameContext()`.
49	
50	```tsx
51	<AnswerGame config={config}>
52	  <AnswerGame.Question />
53	  <AnswerGame.Answer />
54	  <AnswerGame.Choices />
55	</AnswerGame>
56	```
57	
58	`AnswerGame.Question/Answer/Choices` render their `children` inside the
59	correct layout zone. When used without children they render nothing (useful
60	for games that omit a zone, e.g. no choices in type-only mode).
61	
62	### Slot naming
63	
64	| Slot       | Purpose                                                                        |
65	| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
66	| `Question` | What is being asked — any question primitives from `src/components/questions/` |
67	| `Answer`   | Where the answer goes — drag targets or typed slots (input-method agnostic)    |
68	| `Choices`  | Source tiles the child picks from — hidden/absent in type-only mode            |
69	
70	### Compound parts (layout wrappers only)
71	
72	```tsx
73	// AnswerGame.tsx
74	AnswerGame.Question = ({ children }) => (
75	  <div className="game-question-zone">{children}</div>
76	);
77	AnswerGame.Answer = ({ children }) => (
78	  <div className="game-answer-zone">{children}</div>
79	);
80	AnswerGame.Choices = ({ children }) => (
81	  <div className="game-choices-zone">{children}</div>
82	);
83	```
84	
85	### How different games compose it
86	
87	```tsx
88	// WordSpell
89	function WordSpell({ config }: { config: WordSpellConfig }) {
90	  return (
91	    <AnswerGame config={config}>
92	      <AnswerGame.Question>
93	        {config.mode !== 'recall' && <ImageQuestion />}
94	        <AudioButton />
95	      </AnswerGame.Question>
96	      <AnswerGame.Answer>
97	        <OrderedLetterSlots />
98	      </AnswerGame.Answer>
99	      <AnswerGame.Choices>
100	        <LetterTileBank />
101	      </AnswerGame.Choices>
102	    </AnswerGame>
103	  );
104	}
105	
106	// NumberMatch
107	function NumberMatch({ config }: { config: NumberMatchConfig }) {
108	  return (
109	    <AnswerGame config={config}>
110	      <AnswerGame.Question>
111	        {config.mode.startsWith('numeral') ||
112	        config.mode === 'word-to-numeral' ? (
113	          <TextQuestion />
114	        ) : (
115	          <DotGroupQuestion />
116	        )}
117	        <AudioButton />
118	      </AnswerGame.Question>
119	      <AnswerGame.Answer>
120	        <MatchingPairZones />
121	      </AnswerGame.Answer>
122	      <AnswerGame.Choices>
123	        <NumeralTileBank />
124	      </AnswerGame.Choices>
125	    </AnswerGame>
126	  );
127	}
128	
129	// SortNumbers (future)
130	function SortNumbers({ config }: { config: SortNumbersConfig }) {
131	  return (
132	    <AnswerGame config={config}>
133	      <AnswerGame.Question>
134	        <TextQuestion />
135	        <AudioButton />
136	      </AnswerGame.Question>
137	      <AnswerGame.Answer>
138	        <SortableSequence />
139	      </AnswerGame.Answer>
140	      <AnswerGame.Choices>
141	        <NumberTileBank />
142	      </AnswerGame.Choices>
143	    </AnswerGame>
144	  );
145	}
146	```
147	
148	---
149	
150	## 4. Question Primitives
151	
152	Shared question primitives live at `src/components/questions/`. Each is
153	**prop-free** — they read config from `useAnswerGameContext()`. Any game can
154	use any combination.
155	
156	### Primitives
157	
158	| Component          | Renders                                        | Use case                              |
159	| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- |
160	| `ImageQuestion`    | An image (Fluent Emoji or custom asset)        | Word picture mode, scene illustration |
161	| `AudioButton`      | A speaker button that triggers TTS             | Explicit "hear it" affordance         |
162	| `TextQuestion`     | Styled text (word, numeral, sentence with gap) | Recall mode, numeral-to-word          |
163	| `DotGroupQuestion` | Dot/object cluster visual                      | NumberMatch quantity display          |
164	
165	### TTS on tap
166	
167	All question primitives are **tappable-to-TTS by default**. `AudioButton`
168	is an explicit affordance for the same action. All call `useGameTTS()`,
169	which delegates to the `SpeechOutput` service (M2). `SpeechOutput` honours
170	the parent's TTS on/off setting per profile (PRD §5.6, user story C-10) —
171	no prop needed on the primitives.
172	
173	```tsx
174	// Parent has TTS enabled → image tap and button both speak
175	// Parent has TTS disabled → both silently no-op
176	<AnswerGame.Question>
177	  <ImageQuestion /> {/* tappable → useGameTTS() */}
178	  <AudioButton /> {/* button → useGameTTS() */}
179	</AnswerGame.Question>
180	```
181	
182	### File structure
183	
184	```
185	src/components/questions/
186	  ImageQuestion.tsx
187	  AudioButton.tsx
188	  TextQuestion.tsx
189	  DotGroupQuestion.tsx
190	  index.ts
191	  questions.stories.tsx
192	```
193	
194	---
195	
196	## 5. Interaction Modes
197	
198	### 5.1 Auto-Next-Slot (default)
199	
200	- Tiles fill answer zones left-to-right automatically.
201	- Child can **tap** a tile (fires into next slot) **or drag** it.
202	- Tapping an already-placed tile in a slot removes it and returns it to
203	  choices (undo — drag/tap mode only; backspace is the undo in type mode).
204	- Active slot pulses (glowing ring + cursor blink) to guide the child.
205	
206	### 5.2 Free-Swap
207	
208	- Child drags to any slot; occupied slots swap tiles.
209	- Already-placed tiles can be dragged directly between slots.
210	- Used by scramble-style games (e.g. WordSpell scramble mode, SortNumbers).
211	
212	### 5.3 Type Mode (`inputMethod: "type"`)
213	
214	- Choices hidden; slots show faint letter hints.
215	- Keystrokes fill slots left-to-right with a spring-in animation.
216	- Backspace removes the last placed tile.
217	- Wrong key: slot flashes red.
218	- Compatible with all game modes.
219	
220	### 5.4 Magnetic Snap (drag only)
221	
222	CSS lerp-based pull activates within 60 px of any valid answer zone:
223	
224	```
225	if (distanceToZoneCenter < 60px) {
226	  t = 1 - (distance / 60);
227	  itemX = lerp(currentX, zoneCenter.x, t * 0.3);
228	  itemY = lerp(currentY, zoneCenter.y, t * 0.3);
229	}
230	```
231	
232	---
233	
234	## 6. TTS Triggers
235	
236	| Trigger                                  | Behaviour                                    |
237	| ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
238	| Tile tap / drag-start                    | TTS speaks tile label immediately (no delay) |
239	| Tap on `ImageQuestion` or `TextQuestion` | TTS reads the full prompt                    |
240	| `AudioButton` press                      | TTS reads the full prompt                    |
241	| Hint button (header)                     | TTS reads the full prompt                    |
242	| Wrong answer                             | TTS reads mascot encouragement message       |
243	| Round start (`inputMethod: "recall"`)    | TTS auto-reads the prompt                    |
244	
245	---
246	
247	## 7. Wrong-Tile Behaviour
248	
249	Configurable via `wrongTileBehavior`:
250	
251	| Value             | Behaviour                                                          |
252	| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
253	| `lock-auto-eject` | Tile locks red → auto-returns to choices after ~1 000 ms (default) |
254	| `reject`          | Tile bounces back immediately, slot never fills                    |
255	| `lock-manual`     | Tile stays red; child drags it back manually                       |
256	
257	On wrong placement: koala mascot appears with a supportive message, TTS reads it, then fades after 2 s.
258	
259	---
260	
261	## 8. Celebration
262	
263	### Per-round (inline)
264	
265	`ScoreAnimation` (M4): confetti + stars, CSS-only, ~2 s. Auto-advances to next round.
266	
267	### Game-over (all rounds complete)
268	
269	Full-screen overlay:
270	
271	- Stars rain down (CSS animation)
272	- Animated koala mascot (inline SVG + CSS keyframe dance)
273	- Score stars (1–5) based on retry count
274	- Jingle plays once on enter (~2–3 s, CC0 audio asset)
275	- Buttons: "Play again" (restart same config) | "Home" (dashboard)
276	
277	---
278	
279	## 9. Layout
280	
281	Stacked, portrait-first (works on landscape tablet and desktop):
282	
283	```
284	┌──────────────────────────────┐
285	│ [Exit]  ● ● ○ ○ ○  [Hint 🔊] │  ← Game shell header
286	├──────────────────────────────┤
287	│       {Question slot}        │  ← game-supplied question primitives
288	│       {Answer slot}          │  ← game-supplied answer zones
289	├──────────────────────────────┤
290	│       {Choices slot}         │  ← game-supplied tiles (hidden in type mode)
291	└──────────────────────────────┘
292	```
293	
294	- Header: Exit (left), progress dots — gold = complete, grey = pending (centre), Hint TTS (right)
295	- Minimum touch target: 52×56 px per tile and slot
296	
297	---
298	
299	## 10. Base Config Type
300	
301	```ts
302	// src/components/answer-game/types.ts
303	interface AnswerGameConfig {
304	  gameId: string;
305	  inputMethod: 'drag' | 'type' | 'both'; // default: 'drag'
306	  wrongTileBehavior: 'reject' | 'lock-manual' | 'lock-auto-eject'; // default: 'lock-auto-eject'
307	  tileBankMode: 'exact' | 'distractors'; // default: 'exact'
308	  distractorCount?: number; // when tileBankMode='distractors'
309	}
310	```
311	
312	Game-specific configs extend this base. See the WordSpell + NumberMatch spec for examples.
313	
314	---
315	
316	## 11. File Structure
317	
318	Each UI component lives in its own directory alongside its test and story files.
319	Module-level files (hooks, provider, types) stay flat in the module directory.
320	
321	```
322	src/components/answer-game/
323	  AnswerGame/
324	    AnswerGame.tsx              ← composition root, named slot wrappers, layout
325	    AnswerGame.stories.tsx
326	  AnswerGameProvider.tsx        ← context: drag state, evaluation, TTS
327	  useAnswerGameContext.ts        ← read shared game state
328	  useAnswerGameDispatch.ts       ← fire evaluation actions
329	  useTileEvaluation.ts           ← wrong-tile behaviour, auto-eject timer
330	  useGameTTS.ts                  ← TTS triggers
331	  useAutoNextSlot.ts             ← auto-next-slot interaction
332	  useFreeSwap.ts                 ← free-swap interaction
333	  types.ts                       ← AnswerGameConfig, TileItem, AnswerZone
334	
335	src/components/questions/
336	  ImageQuestion/
337	    ImageQuestion.tsx
338	    ImageQuestion.test.tsx
339	    ImageQuestion.stories.tsx
340	  AudioButton/
341	    AudioButton.tsx
342	    AudioButton.test.tsx
343	    AudioButton.stories.tsx
344	  TextQuestion/
345	    TextQuestion.tsx
346	    TextQuestion.test.tsx
347	    TextQuestion.stories.tsx
348	  DotGroupQuestion/
349	    DotGroupQuestion.tsx
350	    DotGroupQuestion.test.tsx
351	    DotGroupQuestion.stories.tsx
352	  index.ts
353	```
354	
355	---
356	
357	## 12. Accessibility
358	
359	- All tiles: `role="button"`, `aria-label="Letter A"` (or tile label).
360	- Answer zones container: `role="list"`, `aria-label="Answer slots"`.
361	- Each zone: `role="listitem"`, `aria-label="Slot 1, empty"` / `"Slot 1, filled with C"`.
362	- `aria-grabbed="true"` on dragged tile; `aria-dropeffect="move"` on valid zones.
363	- `ImageQuestion`: `role="button"`, `aria-label="[word] — tap to hear"`.
364	- `TextQuestion`: `role="button"`, `aria-label="[text] — tap to hear"`.
365	- `AudioButton`: `role="button"`, `aria-label="Hear the question"`.
366	- Keyboard drag simulation (per [ui-ux.md §8.5](../../ui-ux.md)):
367	  Space/Enter to pick up → Arrow keys to move → Space/Enter to drop → Escape to cancel.
368	- `prefers-reduced-motion`: disables scale animations; confetti replaced by a static star badge.
369	- All TTS via `SpeechOutput` service (M2) — honours parent voice selection and TTS on/off.
370	- Minimum contrast: tile labels 4.5:1 on tile background.
371	
372	---
373	
374	## 13. Session Recording
375	
376	Standard M4 game events — no custom recording logic needed:
377	
378	| Event                     | When                                      |
379	| ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
380	| `game:start`              | Component mounts with config              |
381	| `game:instructions_shown` | TTS prompt fires on round start           |
382	| `game:action`             | Every tile placement attempt              |
383	| `game:evaluate`           | Per-tile evaluation result                |
384	| `game:hint`               | Hint TTS button tapped or question tapped |
385	| `game:score`              | Round completed                           |
386	| `game:retry`              | Wrong tile ejected                        |
387	| `game:end`                | All rounds complete or child exits        |
388	
389	Payload includes: `{ tileValue, slotIndex, roundIndex, inputMethod, correct }`.
390	
391	---
392	
393	## 14. Testing
394	
395	| Layer       | What to test                                                                    |
396	| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
397	| Unit        | Magnetic snap lerp calculation at boundary values                               |
398	| Unit        | Auto-eject timer fires after 1 000 ms                                           |
399	| Unit        | Auto-next-slot advances on correct placement, stays on wrong                    |
400	| Unit        | Free-swap exchanges two occupied slots                                          |
401	| Unit        | TTS fires on tile tap, question tap, and AudioButton; not on drag-cancel        |
402	| Unit        | TTS no-ops when SpeechOutput service has TTS disabled                           |
403	| Integration | Full round via GameEngineProvider: place tiles → complete → next round          |
404	| Integration | Wrong-tile all three behaviours                                                 |
405	| Integration | Keyboard drag simulation: pick up → move → drop                                 |
406	| Storybook   | All interaction modes × wrong-tile behaviours                                   |
407	| Storybook   | Question primitives: ImageQuestion, TextQuestion, DotGroupQuestion, AudioButton |
408	| Storybook   | Game-over celebration screen (koala + stars)                                    |
409
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-02 03:32:02Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-13-mobile-resilience.md`

```text
1	# Mobile Resilience Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use
4	> superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or
5	> superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use
6	> checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
7	
8	**Goal:** Fix dark mode colour derivation, guard against iOS Brave voice
9	crashes, and add layered error boundaries to prevent white-screen failures.
10	
11	**Architecture:** Three independent fixes in a single branch. Dark mode fixes
12	the `.dark` CSS block to derive from `--bs-*` tokens. Voice crash is solved by
13	a shared `safeGetVoices` helper. Error handling adds a global React
14	`ErrorBoundary` + TanStack Router's `defaultErrorComponent`.
15	
16	**Tech Stack:** CSS custom properties, Web Speech API, React class components,
17	TanStack Router `ErrorComponentProps`
18	
19	---
20	
21	## File Structure
22	
23	| Action | Path                                                             | Responsibility                                      |
24	| ------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
25	| Modify | `src/styles.css`                                                 | `.dark` block: derive from `--bs-*` tokens          |
26	| Modify | `src/components/questions/DotGroupQuestion/DotGroupQuestion.tsx` | Replace `text-white` with `text-primary-foreground` |
27	| Create | `src/lib/speech/safe-get-voices.ts`                              | Shared `safeGetVoices` helper                       |
28	| Create | `src/lib/speech/safe-get-voices.test.ts`                         | Tests for `safeGetVoices`                           |
29	| Modify | `src/lib/speech/SpeechOutput.ts`                                 | Use `safeGetVoices`                                 |
30	| Modify | `src/lib/speech/voices.ts`                                       | Use `safeGetVoices`                                 |
31	| Modify | `src/routes/$locale/_app/settings.tsx`                           | Use `safeGetVoices`                                 |
32	| Create | `src/components/RouteErrorFallback.tsx`                          | Shared error fallback UI                            |
33	| Create | `src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx`                               | Global React error boundary                         |
34	| Modify | `src/routes/__root.tsx`                                          | Wrap content in `ErrorBoundary`                     |
35	| Modify | `src/router.tsx`                                                 | Set `defaultErrorComponent`                         |
36	
37	---
38	
39	### Task 1: Fix `.dark` CSS block to derive from `--bs-*` tokens
40	
41	**Files:**
42	
43	- Modify: `src/styles.css:518-564`
44	
45	- [ ] **Step 1: Replace hardcoded `.dark` values with `var(--bs-*, fallback)`**
46	
47	In `src/styles.css`, replace the `.dark` block (lines 518-564) with:
48	
49	```css
50	.dark {
51	  /*
52	   * Derive from --bs-* tokens with dark-mode fallbacks.
53	   * --bs-* vars are set as inline styles on <html> by ThemeRuntimeProvider
54	   * and by the Storybook withTheme decorator. The fallback values match the
55	   * Galaxy dark palette for when no inline --bs-* vars are present.
56	   */
57	  --background: var(--bs-background, oklch(14.5% 0 0deg));
58	  --foreground: var(--bs-text, oklch(98.5% 0 0deg));
59	  --card: var(--bs-surface, oklch(20.5% 0 0deg));
60	  --card-foreground: var(--bs-text, oklch(98.5% 0 0deg));
61	  --popover: var(--bs-surface, oklch(20.5% 0 0deg));
62	  --popover-foreground: var(--bs-text, oklch(98.5% 0 0deg));
63	  --primary: var(--bs-primary, oklch(92.2% 0 0deg));
64	  --primary-foreground: var(--bs-surface, oklch(20.5% 0 0deg));
65	  --secondary: var(--bs-secondary, oklch(26.9% 0 0deg));
66	  --secondary-foreground: var(--bs-surface, oklch(98.5% 0 0deg));
67	  --muted: var(--bs-surface, oklch(26.9% 0 0deg));
68	  --muted-foreground: oklch(70.8% 0 0deg);
69	  --accent: var(--bs-accent, oklch(26.9% 0 0deg));
70	  --accent-foreground: var(--bs-text, oklch(98.5% 0 0deg));
71	  --destructive: oklch(70.4% 0.191 22.216deg);
72	  --border: oklch(100% 0 0deg / 10%);
73	  --input: oklch(100% 0 0deg / 15%);
74	  --ring: var(--bs-primary, oklch(55.6% 0 0deg));
75	  --chart-1: oklch(87% 0 0deg);
76	  --chart-2: oklch(55.6% 0 0deg);
77	  --chart-3: oklch(43.9% 0 0deg);
78	  --chart-4: oklch(37.1% 0 0deg);
79	  --chart-5: oklch(26.9% 0 0deg);
80	  --sidebar: var(--bs-surface, oklch(20.5% 0 0deg));
81	  --sidebar-foreground: var(--bs-text, oklch(98.5% 0 0deg));
82	  --sidebar-primary: oklch(48.8% 0.243 264.376deg);
83	  --sidebar-primary-foreground: oklch(98.5% 0 0deg);
84	  --sidebar-accent: var(--bs-surface, oklch(26.9% 0 0deg));
85	  --sidebar-accent-foreground: var(--bs-text, oklch(98.5% 0 0deg));
86	  --sidebar-border: oklch(100% 0 0deg / 10%);
87	  --sidebar-ring: oklch(55.6% 0 0deg);
88	
89	  /* Skeuomorphic tile tokens — dark */
90	  --skeuo-highlight: rgb(255 255 255 / 6%);
91	  --skeuo-ring: rgb(255 255 255 / 10%);
92	  --skeuo-inset-bottom: rgb(255 255 255 / 4%);
93	  --skeuo-inset-top: rgb(255 255 255 / 8%);
94	  --skeuo-text-shadow: rgb(0 0 0 / 40%);
95	}
96	```

205	    } as unknown as SpeechSynthesis;
206	    expect(safeGetVoices(synth)).toEqual([voice]);

208

213	    } as SpeechSynthesisVoice;

215	    const synth = {

217	    } as unknown as SpeechSynthesis;
218	    expect(safeGetVoices(synth)).toEqual([good]);
219	  });
220

223	      getVoices: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => {

225	      }),
226	    } as unknown as SpeechSynthesis;

228	  });
229

238	
239	- [ ] **Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail**
240	
241	Run: `npx vitest run src/lib/speech/safe-get-voices.test.ts`
242	Expected: FAIL — module `./safe-get-voices` not found
243	
244	- [ ] **Step 3: Write the implementation**
245	
246	Create `src/lib/speech/safe-get-voices.ts`:
247	
248	```ts
249	/**
250	 * Safely retrieve voices from SpeechSynthesis.
251	 *
252	 * iOS Brave returns broken/undefined voice objects from getVoices().
253	 * This helper filters out null, undefined, and entries without a valid
254	 * `.name` property, and catches getVoices() throwing entirely.
255	 */
256	export const safeGetVoices = (
257	  synth: SpeechSynthesis,
258	): SpeechSynthesisVoice[] => {
259	  try {
260	    return synth
261	      .getVoices()
262	      .filter(
263	        (v): v is SpeechSynthesisVoice =>
264	          v != null && typeof v.name === 'string',
265	      );
266	  } catch {
267	    return [];
268	  }
269	};
270	```
271	
272	- [ ] **Step 4: Run tests to verify they pass**
273	
274	Run: `npx vitest run src/lib/speech/safe-get-voices.test.ts`
275	Expected: All 6 tests PASS

280	git add src/lib/speech/safe-get-voices.ts src/lib/speech/safe-get-voices.test.ts
281	git commit -m "feat(speech): add safeGetVoices helper for iOS Brave
282	
283	iOS Brave exposes speechSynthesis but returns broken voice objects.
284	This helper filters out null/undefined entries and catches throws."
285	```

459	### Task 7: Create `RouteErrorFallback` component
460	
461	**Files:**

463	- Create: `src/components/RouteErrorFallback.tsx`
464	
465	- [ ] **Step 1: Create the component**
466	
467	Create `src/components/RouteErrorFallback.tsx`:
468	
469	```tsx
470	import type { ErrorComponentProps } from '@tanstack/react-router';
471	
472	interface RouteErrorFallbackProps {
473	  error: unknown;
474	  onRetry: () => void;
475	}
476	
477	const ErrorFallbackContent = ({
478	  error,
479	  onRetry,
480	}: RouteErrorFallbackProps) => (
481	  <div className="flex min-h-[50vh] flex-col items-center justify-center gap-4 px-4 text-center">
482	    <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-foreground">
483	      Something went wrong
484	    </h1>
485	    <p className="max-w-md text-muted-foreground">
486	      An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
487	    </p>
488	    {import.meta.env.DEV && error instanceof Error && (
489	      <pre className="max-w-full overflow-auto rounded-lg bg-muted p-4 text-left text-sm text-muted-foreground">
490	        {error.message}
491	      </pre>
492	    )}
493	    <button
494	      type="button"
495	      className="rounded-lg bg-primary px-6 py-2 font-semibold text-primary-foreground"
496	      onClick={onRetry}
497	    >
498	      Try again
499	    </button>
500	  </div>
501	);
502	
503	/** Route-level error component for TanStack Router's defaultErrorComponent. */
504	export const RouteErrorFallback = ({
505	  error,
506	  reset,
507	}: ErrorComponentProps) => (
508	  <ErrorFallbackContent error={error} onRetry={reset} />
509	);
510	
511	/** Global error fallback — used by the top-level ErrorBoundary. */
512	export const GlobalErrorFallback = ({ error }: { error: unknown }) => (
513	  <ErrorFallbackContent
514	    error={error}
515	    onRetry={() => globalThis.location.reload()}
516	  />
517	);
518	```
519

530

537	### Task 8: Create global `ErrorBoundary` component
538	
539	**Files:**
540	
541	- Create: `src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx`
542	
543	- [ ] **Step 1: Create the class component**
544	
545	Create `src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx`:
546	
547	```tsx
548	import { Component } from 'react';
549	import type { ErrorInfo, ReactNode } from 'react';
550	import { GlobalErrorFallback } from './RouteErrorFallback';
551	
552	interface ErrorBoundaryProps {
553	  children: ReactNode;
554	}
555	
556	interface ErrorBoundaryState {
557	  error: unknown | null;
558	}
559	
560	export class ErrorBoundary extends Component<
561	  ErrorBoundaryProps,
562	  ErrorBoundaryState
563	> {
564	  constructor(props: ErrorBoundaryProps) {
565	    super(props);
566	    this.state = { error: null };
567	  }
568	
569	  static getDerivedStateFromError(error: unknown): ErrorBoundaryState {
570	    return { error };
571	  }
572	
573	  componentDidCatch(error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo): void {
574	    console.error('[ErrorBoundary] Uncaught error:', error, errorInfo);
575	  }
576	
577	  render() {
578	    if (this.state.error !== null) {
579	      return <GlobalErrorFallback error={this.state.error} />;
580	    }
581	    return this.props.children;
582	  }
583	}
584	```
585	
586	- [ ] **Step 2: Verify typecheck passes**
587	
588	Run: `yarn typecheck`
589	Expected: PASS

596	
597	Catches unhandled React errors and shows GlobalErrorFallback with a
598	reload button. Prevents full white-screen failures."
599	```

 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-08-drag-preview-sound-fix-design.md`

```text
1	# Drag Preview in Slots + Sound Bug Fix — Design Spec
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Two related improvements to the answer-game drag system:
6	
7	1. **Drag preview** — while dragging a tile over a slot, show a visual preview
8	   of the final result before the user drops (full swap preview on both source
9	   and target slots)
10	2. **Sound bug fix** — correct the sound evaluation when moving a tile from a
11	   slot to an empty slot, and suppress the spurious `tile-place` sound from the
12	   source slot emptying
13	
14	## Feature 1: Drag Preview
15	
16	### State Changes
17	
18	Add to `AnswerGameState` in `types.ts`:
19	
20	```ts
21	dragHoverZoneIndex: number | null;
22	```
23	
24	Add to `AnswerGameAction`:
25	
26	```ts
27	| { type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER'; zoneIndex: number | null }
28	```
29	
30	The reducer sets `dragHoverZoneIndex` on `SET_DRAG_HOVER`. Initialized as
31	`null`, cleared on any drop/cancel alongside `SET_DRAG_ACTIVE` clearing.
32	
33	**Belt-and-suspenders:** when `SET_DRAG_ACTIVE` sets `tileId` to `null`, the
34	reducer also clears `dragHoverZoneIndex` to `null`.
35	
36	**Not persisted** — `buildDraft()` in `useAnswerGameDraftSync` (PR #29)
37	cherry-picks fields explicitly, so `dragHoverZoneIndex` is excluded
38	automatically.
39	
40	### Hover Detection
41	
42	#### HTML5 DnD Path
43	
44	In `useSlotBehavior.ts`, extend the `dropTargetForElements` registration:
45	
46	- `onDragEnter`: dispatch `SET_DRAG_HOVER` with the slot's zone index
47	- `onDragLeave`: dispatch `SET_DRAG_HOVER` with `null`
48	
49	#### Touch Path
50	
51	In `useTouchDrag.ts`, add a new callback to `UseTouchDragOptions`:
52	
53	```ts
54	onHoverZone?: (zoneIndex: number | null) => void;
55	```
56	
57	During `onPointerMove`, after the drag threshold is crossed, use
58	`elementsFromPoint` to find the zone under the pointer. Call `onHoverZone`
59	when the detected zone changes (track via a `lastHoverZoneRef` to avoid
60	redundant calls).
61	
62	#### Cleanup
63	
64	`SET_DRAG_HOVER` with `null` is dispatched on:
65	
66	- `onDragLeave` (HTML5)
67	- `onDrop` / `onDragCancel` (both paths)
68	- `SET_DRAG_ACTIVE` clearing to `null` (auto-clear in reducer)
69	
70	### Preview Rendering
71	
72	#### Computed Preview State in useSlotBehavior
73	
74	Each slot derives its preview state from context:
75	
76	- `isPreviewTarget`: `dragHoverZoneIndex === index && dragActiveTileId !== null`
77	- `isPreviewSource`: this slot is the source of the active drag AND
78	  `dragHoverZoneIndex !== null` (i.e. the drag is hovering over some target)
79	
80	Preview logic per scenario:
81	
82	| Scenario             | Target slot shows  | Source slot shows      |
83	| -------------------- | ------------------ | ---------------------- |
84	| Bank → empty slot    | Dragged tile label | N/A (bank, not a slot) |
85	| Bank → occupied slot | Dragged tile label | N/A (bank, not a slot) |
86	| Slot → empty slot    | Dragged tile label | Empty                  |
87	| Slot → occupied slot | Dragged tile label | Target's current tile  |
88	
89	#### New SlotRenderProps Fields
90	
91	```ts
92	isPreview: boolean; // true when showing preview content
93	previewLabel: string | null; // label to render during preview
94	```
95	
96	#### Visual Treatment in Slot.tsx
97	
98	When `isPreview` is true:
99	
100	- Border switches to `border-dashed`
101	- Tile label renders at `opacity-50`
102	- Slot gets a pulsing ring animation (CSS keyframes):
103	
104	```css
105	@keyframes pulseRing {
106	  0%,
107	  100% {
108	    box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px hsla(221, 83%, 53%, 0.15);
109	  }
110	  50% {
111	    box-shadow: 0 0 0 5px hsla(221, 83%, 53%, 0.25);
112	  }
113	}
114	```
115	
116	When `isPreview` is false, rendering is unchanged.
117	
118	## Feature 2: Sound Bug Fix
119	
120	### Root Cause
121	
122	In `useSlotBehavior.ts`, `handleDrop` always dispatches `SWAP_TILES` for
123	slot-to-slot drags. Two bugs:
124	
125	1. When the target is empty (a move, not a swap), `displacedTile` is null, so
126	   `displacedCorrect` defaults to `true` — plays "correct" even when the moved
127	   tile is wrong at the target
128	2. The animation effect (lines 201-203) fires `tile-place` when
129	   `tileId === null && prevTileId !== null` in the source slot, overriding the
130	   swap/move sound
131	
132	### Fix
133	
134	#### 1. handleDrop Sound Evaluation
135	
136	When `displacedTile` is null (move to empty slot): evaluate only the moved
137	tile against the target zone's `expectedValue`. Play correct/wrong based on
138	that alone.
139	
140	When both tiles exist (true swap): keep current logic — correct if either
141	lands correctly, wrong only if both are wrong.
142	
143	#### 2. Suppress tile-place on Source Emptying During Swap/Move
144	
145	The animation effect fires when `tileId === null && prevTileId !== null`.
146	Add a guard: skip the `tile-place` sound when the slot emptied because of a
147	`SWAP_TILES` dispatch. Track this by checking if `dragActiveTileId` was set
148	when the transition happened — if the tile left during an active drag, it is
149	a swap/move, not a manual removal.
150	
151	#### 3. Animate Both Slots on Swap/Move
152	
153	The existing swap animation (lines 169-179) handles the target. For the
154	source slot emptying during a swap/move: skip animation (no pop, no shake).
155	The source slot simply becomes empty or receives the swapped tile.
156	
157	### Sound Rules
158	
159	| Scenario                          | Sound        | Animation                                |
160	| --------------------------------- | ------------ | ---------------------------------------- |
161	| Swap, either tile correct         | `correct`    | Both slots: pop or shake per correctness |
162	| Swap, both tiles wrong            | `wrong`      | Both slots: shake                        |
163	| Move to empty slot, correct       | `correct`    | Target: pop                              |
164	| Move to empty slot, wrong         | `wrong`      | Target: shake                            |
165	| Return to bank                    | `tile-place` | Source: existing eject flow              |
166	| Source empties (during swap/move) | _none_       | _none_                                   |
167	
168	## Compatibility with PR #29
169	
170	PR #29 (service worker + game session resume) adds `AnswerGameDraftState` to
171	`types.ts` and `useAnswerGameDraftSync` for state persistence. This spec is
172	fully compatible:
173	
174	- `dragHoverZoneIndex` is transient state, not included in `buildDraft()`
175	  (which cherry-picks fields)
176	- No overlapping file changes — PR #29 touches `AnswerGameProvider.tsx` and
177	  game components; this spec touches `useSlotBehavior.ts`, `Slot.tsx`,
178	  `answer-game-reducer.ts`, `useSlotTileDrag.ts`, and `useTouchDrag.ts`
179	- Types additions are in different sections of `types.ts` (clean merge)
180	
181	## Testing
182	
183	### Unit Tests
184	
185	- `answer-game-reducer.test.ts` — `SET_DRAG_HOVER` action: sets
186	  `dragHoverZoneIndex`, clears on null, auto-clears when `SET_DRAG_ACTIVE`
187	  goes null
188	- `useSlotBehavior` sound tests — verify all six sound scenarios from the
189	  table above
190	
191	### Storybook Interaction Tests
192	
193	- Stories exercising drag preview states: slot in preview-target mode (dashed
194	  border, faded label, glow), slot in preview-source mode (showing incoming
195	  swap tile), and idle slot (unchanged)
196	- Visual state tests — set context values directly, no actual drag needed
197	
198	### Visual Regression Tests
199	
200	- New VR baselines for preview states across all four scenarios (bank→empty,
201	  bank→occupied, slot→empty, slot→swap)
202	- Both light and dark mode if dark mode is active on this branch
203	
204	### Out of Scope
205	
206	- E2E drag tests — drag interactions in Playwright are fragile and the
207	  existing E2E suite does not cover drag. Unit + Storybook + VR provide
208	  sufficient coverage.
209	
210	## Key Files
211	
212	| File                                  | Changes                                        |
213	| ------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
214	| `answer-game/types.ts`                | Add `dragHoverZoneIndex` to state, new action  |
215	| `answer-game/answer-game-reducer.ts`  | Handle `SET_DRAG_HOVER`, auto-clear logic      |
216	| `answer-game/Slot/useSlotBehavior.ts` | Preview derivation, hover dispatch, sound fix  |
217	| `answer-game/Slot/Slot.tsx`           | Preview visual treatment                       |
218	| `answer-game/useSlotTileDrag.ts`      | Pass hover callbacks to touch drag             |
219	| `answer-game/useTouchDrag.ts`         | `onHoverZone` callback, zone detection on move |
220
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-02 03:32:09Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-07-unified-slot-component-design.md`

```text
1	# Unified Slot Component Design
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-04-07
4	**Status:** Approved
5	**Approach:** New component, parallel migration (Approach A)
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	The answer-game framework has two slot components that serve nearly identical purposes:
10	
11	- `OrderedSlots` (used by WordSpell and SortNumbers) — auto-advancing sequential slots with keyboard input support
12	- `MatchingPairZones` (used by NumberMatch) — free-swap slots, drag-only
13	
14	These components share ~80% of their code (drag source, drop target, click-to-remove, state styling) but diverge on interaction mode and sizing. This duplication blocks reuse for upcoming features (scramble mode, sentence-gap) and makes it harder to deliver consistent animations and drag behavior across games.
15	
16	Additionally, the current drag system has a mobile bug (frozen ghost tiles) and lacks visual polish (no feedback animations, inconsistent drag effects between bank↔slot directions).
17	
18	## Goals
19	
20	1. **Unify** `OrderedSlots` and `MatchingPairZones` into a single `<Slot>` compound component
21	2. **Add feedback animations** — shake on wrong, pop/bounce on correct, animated auto-eject return
22	3. **Fix mobile drag bug** — frozen ghost tiles when touch gestures are interrupted
23	4. **Skeuomorphic drag** — consistent "lift + hole" effect in both directions (bank→slot, slot→bank)
24	5. **Dynamic slot sizing** — slots adapt to content (letters, dice, dominoes, words)
25	6. **Sentence-gap support** — inline droppable gaps within sentence text, with multi-gap support
26	
27	## 1. Unified Slot Component
28	
29	### Location
30	
31	`src/components/answer-game/Slot/`
32	
33	### Pattern: Compound Component with Render Prop
34	
35	The `<Slot>` component owns all behavior; the consumer controls visual content via a render function.
36	
37	```tsx
38	// WordSpell — small square letter slots
39	<SlotRow className="gap-2">
40	  {zones.map((zone, i) => (
41	    <Slot key={zone.id} index={i} className="size-14 rounded-lg">
42	      {({ label }) => <span className="text-2xl font-bold">{label}</span>}
43	    </Slot>
44	  ))}
45	</SlotRow>
46	
47	// NumberMatch — dice content inside larger slots
48	<SlotRow className="gap-4">
49	  {zones.map((zone, i) => (
50	    <Slot key={zone.id} index={i} className="size-20 rounded-2xl">
51	      {({ label }) =>
52	        label ? <DiceFace value={Number(label)} /> : null
53	      }
54	    </Slot>
55	  ))}
56	</SlotRow>
57	
58	// Sentence-gap — inline slots within text
59	<p className="text-lg leading-relaxed">
60	  The{' '}
61	  <Slot index={0} as="span" className="inline-block min-w-16 border-b-2 border-dashed mx-1">
62	    {({ label }) => <span className="text-lg font-bold">{label ?? '\u00A0'}</span>}
63	  </Slot>
64	  {' '}sat on the mat.
65	</p>
66	```
67	
68	### What `<Slot>` Owns (Behavior)
69	
70	- Drop target registration (pragmatic DnD + touch via `useTouchDrag`)
71	- Drag source when filled (for removing/swapping)
72	- Click-to-remove handler
73	- Keyboard input focus (when `inputMethod` includes `'type'`)
74	- Correct/wrong state styling (border + background colors)
75	- Feedback animations (shake, pop, auto-eject return)
76	- Active slot cursor (blinking underline, only in `ordered` interaction mode)
77	- Accessibility (aria-labels, roles)
78	
79	### What the Consumer Owns (Rendering)
80	
81	- Inner content (letter, number, dice face, word, image) via render prop
82	- Size and shape via `className` (`size-14`, `size-20`, `h-[72px] w-32`, inline)
83	- HTML element via `as` prop (`li` default, `span` for inline gaps)
84	
85	### Render Prop Signature
86	
87	```tsx
88	interface SlotRenderProps {
89	  label: string | null; // placed tile's label, or null if empty
90	  tileId: string | null; // placed tile's ID
91	  isActive: boolean; // this slot is the current auto-advance target
92	  isWrong: boolean; // placed tile is incorrect
93	  isLocked: boolean; // tile is locked (wrong + lock mode)
94	  isEmpty: boolean; // no tile placed
95	  showCursor: boolean; // should show blinking cursor (type/both mode + ordered)
96	}
97	
98	interface SlotProps {
99	  index: number;
100	  as?: 'li' | 'span' | 'div';
101	  className?: string;
102	  children: (props: SlotRenderProps) => React.ReactNode;
103	}
104	```
105	
106	### `SlotRow` Wrapper
107	
108	Optional layout wrapper for row-based slot arrangements:
109	
110	```tsx
111	interface SlotRowProps {
112	  className?: string;
113	  children: React.ReactNode;
114	}
115	
116	// Renders <ol> with flex layout, role="list"
117	```
118	
119	### Config: `slotInteraction` (New Field)
120	
121	Added to `AnswerGameConfig`:
122	
123	```typescript
124	interface AnswerGameConfig {
125	  // ... existing fields ...
126	  slotInteraction: 'ordered' | 'free-swap';
127	}
128	```
129	
130	- **`ordered`**: Active slot highlighted, auto-advances on placement. Used for letter-spelling (picture, recall modes).
131	- **`free-swap`**: No active slot, tiles can be swapped between any slots. Used for scramble, NumberMatch, SortNumbers.
132	- **Default**: `'free-swap'` when `inputMethod` is `'drag'` or `'both'`; `'ordered'` when `inputMethod` is `'type'`.
133	
134	## 2. Feedback Animations
135	
136	### Wrong Placement — Shake + Red Flash (~400ms)
137	
138	1. Tile lands in slot → `wrong.mp3` plays
139	2. Slot border/bg turns `border-destructive bg-destructive/10 text-destructive`
140	3. CSS `animate-shake`: horizontal oscillation (±4px, 3 cycles, 300ms)
141	4. Brief red flash overlay fades out (100ms)
142	5. Behavior continues per `wrongTileBehavior` setting
143	
144	### Correct Placement — Pop/Bounce (~250ms)
145	
146	1. Tile lands in slot → `correct.mp3` plays
147	2. Slot scales up (`scale(1.08)`) then settles back (`scale(1)`) — spring-like ease
148	3. Border/bg transitions to `border-primary bg-primary/10 text-primary`
149	
150	### CSS Keyframes
151	
152	```css
153	@keyframes shake {
154	  0%,
155	  100% {
156	    transform: translateX(0);
157	  }
158	  20% {
159	    transform: translateX(-4px);
160	  }
161	  40% {
162	    transform: translateX(4px);
163	  }
164	  60% {
165	    transform: translateX(-3px);
166	  }
167	  80% {
168	    transform: translateX(2px);
169	  }
170	}
171	
172	@keyframes pop {
173	  0% {
174	    transform: scale(1);
175	  }
176	  50% {
177	    transform: scale(1.08);
178	  }
179	  100% {
180	    transform: scale(1);
181	  }
182	}
183	```
184	
185	Added to `src/styles.css` alongside existing `animate-blink`.
186	
187	## 3. Skeuomorphic Drag Effect
188	
189	### Lift Effect (Both Directions)
190	
191	When drag starts (from bank OR slot):
192	
193	- **Touch ghost**: `boxShadow: 0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)`, `transform: scale(1.08)`, `opacity: 0.95`
194	- **Desktop**: Custom drag preview via pragmatic DnD `setCustomNativeDragPreview` with matching shadow/scale
195	
196	### "Hole" Left Behind
197	
198	Where the tile was picked up from:
199	
200	- **In bank**: tile spot shows `bg-muted/30` + `shadow-inner` + `rounded-xl` — an indent/depression. Uses existing `dragActiveTileId` state (via `SET_DRAG_ACTIVE` action) to keep the tile's spot visible in the bank instead of removing it from the list.
201	- **In slot**: slot shows dashed border + `bg-muted/20` — slightly darker than normal empty to suggest "something was here"
202	- Hole disappears when tile is dropped (placed or returned)
203	
204	### Consistency
205	
206	Same lifted styling regardless of drag direction (bank→slot or slot→bank), same on desktop and mobile.
207	
208	## 4. Auto-Eject Return Animation
209	
210	### Sequence (~1300ms total)
211	
212	| Time        | What happens                                                                                |
213	| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
214	| 0–300ms     | Shake animation plays (wrong feedback from Section 2)                                       |
215	| 300–1000ms  | Tile sits locked in slot with red styling (existing `AUTO_EJECT_DELAY_MS`)                  |
216	| 1000–1300ms | Tile animates back: slides toward tile bank center, `scale(0.8)`, `opacity: 0.7`, `ease-in` |
217	| 1300ms      | `EJECT_TILE` dispatched, tile reappears in bank at normal size                              |
218	
219	### Implementation
220	
221	- Before ejecting, measure slot position and bank container center
222	- Apply CSS transition with calculated `translate(deltaX, deltaY)` + scale + opacity
223	- On `transitionend` event, dispatch `EJECT_TILE`
224	- **Fallback**: if DOM measurement fails, fade out in place (graceful degradation)
225	
226	## 5. Mobile Drag Bug Fix
227	
228	### Root Cause
229	
230	In `useSlotTileDrag.ts`, `REMOVE_TILE` dispatches immediately on drag start. If the touch gesture is interrupted (cancelled, leaves viewport, multi-touch conflict), the tile is removed from state but the ghost element lingers — creating frozen overlays (visible in the reported iPhone 17 screenshot with tiles 51 and 58).
231	
232	### Fix (Three-Part)
233	
234	1. **Ghost cleanup on all terminal events**: Register handlers for `pointercancel`, `pointerup`, `contextmenu`, `visibilitychange`, and `touchend` — all trigger ghost removal + pointer capture release.
235	
236	2. **Deferred removal**: Instead of dispatching `REMOVE_TILE` on drag start, hold the tile in a visual "dragging" state. Only dispatch removal on successful drop or confirmed drag-end. If cancelled, tile snaps back to its original slot position.
237	
238	3. **Safety net timeout**: If ghost element still exists after 5 seconds, force-clean it and restore tile to its slot. Prevents permanent frozen states from edge cases.
239	
240	### Files Affected
241	
242	- `src/components/answer-game/useTouchDrag.ts` — ghost lifecycle, event cleanup
243	- `src/components/answer-game/useSlotTileDrag.ts` — deferred removal logic
244	
245	## 6. Dynamic Slot Sizing
246	
247	Slot sizing is fully controlled by the consumer via `className`. The `<Slot>` component has no internal size opinions.
248	
249	### Per-Game Sizing
250	
251	| Game                          | Slot className                          | When                               |
252	| ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
253	| WordSpell (letters)           | `size-14 rounded-lg`                    | Always                             |
254	| WordSpell (syllables/words)   | `min-w-14 h-14 px-2 rounded-lg`         | Auto-width for longer content      |
255	| SortNumbers                   | `size-14 rounded-lg`                    | Always                             |
256	| NumberMatch (dice, all ≤ 6)   | `size-20 rounded-2xl`                   | `round.numbers.every(n => n <= 6)` |
257	| NumberMatch (domino, any > 6) | `h-[72px] w-32 rounded-2xl`             | `round.numbers.some(n => n > 6)`   |
258	| Sentence-gap                  | `inline-block min-w-16 px-2 border-b-2` | Always inline                      |
259	
260	### NumberMatch Dice/Domino Rule
261	
262	When **any** number in the round exceeds 6, **all** slots and tiles render in domino (rectangle) format for visual consistency. When all numbers are ≤ 6, all render as dice (square).
263	
264	```tsx
265	const allDice = round.numbers.every((n) => n <= 6);
266	const slotClass = allDice
267	  ? 'size-20 rounded-2xl'
268	  : 'h-[72px] w-32 rounded-2xl';
269	```
270	
271	## 7. Sentence-Gap Schema
272	
273	### Template Syntax
274	
275	Numbered placeholders `{0}`, `{1}`, etc. in the sentence string, mapped to a `gaps` array.
276	
277	### Types
278	
279	```typescript
280	interface GapDefinition {
281	  word: string; // expected answer for this gap
282	  distractors?: string[]; // extra wrong tile choices, per-gap
283	}
284	
285	interface WordSpellRound {
286	  word: string;
287	  emoji?: string;
288	  image?: string;
289	  sceneImage?: string;
290	  sentence?: string; // "The {0} sat on the {1}."
291	  gaps?: GapDefinition[]; // one entry per numbered placeholder
292	  audioOverride?: string;
293	}
294	```
295	
296	### Single Gap
297	
298	```typescript
299	{
300	  sentence: "The {0} sat on the mat.",
301	  gaps: [{ word: "cat", distractors: ["dog", "bat"] }],
302	}
303	// → 1 zone (expectedValue: "cat")
304	// → 3 tiles: ["cat", "dog", "bat"] shuffled
305	```
306	
307	### Multiple Gaps
308	
309	```typescript
310	{
311	  sentence: "The {0} sat on the {1}.",
312	  gaps: [
313	    { word: "cat", distractors: ["dog"] },
314	    { word: "mat", distractors: ["hat"] },
315	  ],
316	}
317	// → 2 zones (expectedValue: "cat", expectedValue: "mat")
318	// → 4 tiles: ["cat", "mat", "dog", "hat"] shuffled
319	```
320	
321	### Rendering
322	
323	A `<SentenceWithGaps>` helper component parses the template and injects `<Slot>` components:
324	
325	```tsx
326	const SentenceWithGaps = ({ sentence }: { sentence: string }) => {
327	  const segments = parseSentenceTemplate(sentence);
328	  // Splits "The {0} sat on the {1}." into:
329	  // [{ type:'text', value:'The ' }, { type:'gap', index:0 }, ...]
330	
331	  return (
332	    <p className="text-lg leading-relaxed">
333	      {segments.map((seg, i) =>
334	        seg.type === 'text' ? (
335	          <span key={i}>{seg.value}</span>
336	        ) : (
337	          <Slot
338	            key={seg.index}
339	            index={seg.index}
340	            as="span"
341	            className="inline-block min-w-16 border-b-2 border-dashed mx-1"
342	          >
343	            {({ label }) => (
344	              <span className="text-lg font-bold">
345	                {label ?? '\u00A0'}
346	              </span>
347	            )}
348	          </Slot>
349	        ),
350	      )}
351	    </p>
352	  );
353	};
354	```
355	
356	### Zone Generation
357	
358	```typescript
359	const buildSentenceGapRound = (gaps: GapDefinition[]) => {
360	  const zones = gaps.map((gap, i) => ({
361	    id: `z${i}`,
362	    index: i,
363	    expectedValue: gap.word,
364	    placedTileId: null,
365	    isWrong: false,
366	    isLocked: false,
367	  }));
368	
369	  const allWords = [
370	    ...gaps.map((g) => g.word),
371	    ...gaps.flatMap((g) => g.distractors ?? []),
372	  ];
373	  const tiles = shuffle(allWords).map((word) => ({
374	    id: nanoid(),
375	    label: word,
376	    value: word,
377	  }));
378	
379	  return { tiles, zones };
380	};
381	```
382	
383	### Backward Compatibility
384	
385	When `gaps` is undefined, fall back to existing behavior: generate zones from `round.word` and render letter-spelling slots below the sentence text.
386	
387	## Migration Strategy
388	
389	**Approach A: New component, parallel migration.**
390	
391	1. Build `<Slot>`, `<SlotRow>`, and `<SentenceWithGaps>` in `src/components/answer-game/Slot/`
392	2. Add `slotInteraction` to `AnswerGameConfig` with backward-compatible defaults
393	3. Migrate games one at a time:
394	   - SortNumbers first (simplest — same as current OrderedSlots)
395	   - WordSpell second (adds keyboard/cursor support)
396	   - NumberMatch third (validates free-swap + dynamic sizing)
397	   - Sentence-gap last (new feature, validates inline rendering)
398	4. After all games migrated, delete `OrderedSlots/` and `MatchingPairZones/`
399	5. Update game-specific re-exports (`OrderedLetterSlots`, `NumberSequenceSlots`) to point to new component or remove
400	
401	## Key Files
402	
403	| File                                                   | Role                                                 |
404	| ------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- |
405	| `src/components/answer-game/Slot/Slot.tsx`             | New unified slot component                           |
406	| `src/components/answer-game/Slot/SlotRow.tsx`          | Row layout wrapper                                   |
407	| `src/components/answer-game/Slot/SentenceWithGaps.tsx` | Template parser + inline slot renderer               |
408	| `src/components/answer-game/Slot/useSlotBehavior.ts`   | Drag/drop/click/keyboard logic hook                  |
409	| `src/components/answer-game/Slot/slot-animations.ts`   | Shake, pop, auto-eject return animation helpers      |
410	| `src/components/answer-game/types.ts`                  | Add `slotInteraction` to config, add `GapDefinition` |
411	| `src/components/answer-game/useTouchDrag.ts`           | Bug fix: ghost cleanup + deferred removal            |
412	| `src/components/answer-game/useSlotTileDrag.ts`        | Bug fix: deferred removal logic                      |
413	| `src/styles.css`                                       | New keyframes: `shake`, `pop`                        |
414	| `src/games/word-spell/types.ts`                        | Add `gaps?: GapDefinition[]` to round type           |
415

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-02 03:32:20Z)**_

Now let me look at the actual `useTouchDrag` source and any related recent specstory entries about this PR's topic area.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-02 03:32:20Z)**_

Now I have the complete picture. Let me write the findings.

Key confirmed findings:

1. **RxDB schema version not bumped** (P1) — `settings.ts` still `version: 0` after adding `tapForgivenessThreshold`. RxDB's Dexie storage validates the schema fingerprint against IndexedDB. Existing users who already have a `version: 0` settings collection stored will get a schema mismatch error on upgrade, or RxDB will silently refuse to read the collection. This requires `version: 1` and a `migrationStrategies: { 1: (doc) => doc }` passthrough in `create-database.ts` since the new field is optional with a default.

2. **`onTapFallback` rejection path missing feedback** (P2) — `onTapFallback` in `useDraggableTile` calls `placeInNextSlot(tile.id)` without inspecting the return value. The `handleClick` path does inspect `result.rejected` and triggers `REJECT_TAP` + `playSound('wrong')` + `flashBankTileRejectFeedback`. The tap-forgiveness path silently drops a wrong tile with no audio or visual feedback.

3. **Coupled optional parameters without type enforcement** (P2) — `tapForgivenessThreshold` and `onTapFallback` are each independently optional, but the feature is a no-op if only one is provided. TypeScript won't catch passing `tapForgivenessThreshold` without `onTapFallback`. This should be a discriminated union or at minimum documented paired requirement.

4. **Ghost removed manually before `cleanup()` in tap path** (P3) — lines 308-309 manually null out `ghostRef` then call `cleanup()` → `cleanupGhost()`. `cleanupGhost` calls `ghostRef.current?.el.remove()` again (already null, safe). But the early manual removal bypasses `cleanupInProgressRef`, meaning if anything synchronously fires `lostpointercapture` between the manual removal and `cleanup()`, it could call `onDragCancelRef.current?.()`. Low probability but structural inconsistency.

5. **`tapForgiveness` `?? 20` in settings.tsx is redundant** (P3) — `useSettings` guarantees `tapForgivenessThreshold` is `number` (not `undefined`) because `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` includes it and the return type is `typeof DEFAULT_SETTINGS & Partial<SettingsDoc>`. The `?? 20` masks this guarantee. Minor clarity issue, no runtime consequence.

 `./src/components/answer-game/useTouchDrag.ts`

```text
1	import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef } from 'react';
2	import type { PointerEvent } from 'react';
3	
4	const DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX = 8;
5	
6	interface GhostInfo {
7	  el: HTMLDivElement;
8	  halfW: number;
9	  halfH: number;
10	}
11	
12	const buildGhost = (
13	  sourceEl: HTMLElement,
14	  label: string,
15	  x: number,
16	  y: number,
17	): GhostInfo => {
18	  const rect = sourceEl.getBoundingClientRect();
19	  const halfW = rect.width / 2;
20	  const halfH = rect.height / 2;
21	  const el = document.createElement('div');
22	  el.textContent = label;
23	  el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
24	  Object.assign(el.style, {
25	    position: 'fixed',
26	    left: `${x - halfW}px`,
27	    top: `${y - halfH}px`,
28	    width: `${rect.width}px`,
29	    height: `${rect.height}px`,
30	    display: 'flex',
31	    alignItems: 'center',
32	    justifyContent: 'center',
33	    borderRadius: '12px',
34	    background: 'var(--card, #fff)',
35	    color: 'var(--card-foreground, #000)',
36	    fontSize: getComputedStyle(sourceEl).fontSize,
37	    fontWeight: 'bold',
38	    boxShadow: '0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)',
39	    pointerEvents: 'none',
40	    zIndex: '9999',
41	    opacity: '0.95',
42	    userSelect: 'none',
43	    transform: 'scale(1.08)',
44	  });
45	  document.body.append(el);
46	  return { el, halfW, halfH };
47	};
48	
49	export interface TouchDragHandlers {
50	  onPointerDown: (e: PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => void;
51	  onPointerMove: (e: PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => void;
52	  onPointerUp: (e: PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => void;
53	  onPointerCancel: (e: PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => void;
54	}
55	
56	interface UseTouchDragOptions {
57	  /** Empty string disables the drag (no pointer capture, no ghost). */
58	  tileId: string;
59	  label: string;
60	  onDragStart?: () => void;
61	  /** Called when the drag is cancelled or the pointer is released with no valid drop zone. */
62	  onDragCancel?: () => void;
63	  onDrop: (tileId: string, zoneIndex: number) => void;
64	  /** Called when the tile is dropped on the bank container (empty hole). */
65	  onDropOnBank?: () => void;
66	  /** Called when the tile is dropped on a specific bank tile hole. */
67	  onDropOnBankTile?: (bankTileId: string) => void;
68	  onHoverZone?: (zoneIndex: number | null) => void;
69	  /** Called when the ghost enters or leaves a bank tile hole during drag. */
70	  onHoverBankTile?: (bankTileId: string | null) => void;
71	}
72	
73	export const useTouchDrag = ({
74	  tileId,
75	  label,
76	  onDragStart,
77	  onDragCancel,
78	  onDrop,
79	  onDropOnBank,
80	  onDropOnBankTile,
81	  onHoverZone,
82	  onHoverBankTile,
83	}: UseTouchDragOptions): TouchDragHandlers => {
84	  const onDragCancelRef = useRef(onDragCancel);
85	  useEffect(() => {
86	    onDragCancelRef.current = onDragCancel;
87	  }, [onDragCancel]);
88	
89	  const onHoverZoneRef = useRef(onHoverZone);
90	  useEffect(() => {
91	    onHoverZoneRef.current = onHoverZone;
92	  }, [onHoverZone]);
93	
94	  const onHoverBankTileRef = useRef(onHoverBankTile);
95	  useEffect(() => {
96	    onHoverBankTileRef.current = onHoverBankTile;
97	  }, [onHoverBankTile]);
98	
99	  const ghostRef = useRef<GhostInfo | null>(null);
100	  const isDragging = useRef(false);
101	  const startPos = useRef<{ x: number; y: number } | null>(null);
102	  const capturedElRef = useRef<HTMLElement | null>(null);
103	  const capturedPointerIdRef = useRef<number | null>(null);
104	  const safetyTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<
105	    typeof globalThis.setTimeout
106	  > | null>(null);
107	
108	  // Guards against cleanupGhost → releasePointerCapture → lostpointercapture
109	  // → cleanupGhost re-entry.
110	  const cleanupInProgressRef = useRef(false);
111	  const lastHoverZoneRef = useRef<number | null>(null);
112	  const lastHoverBankTileRef = useRef<string | null>(null);
113	
114	  const cleanupGhost = useCallback(() => {
115	    if (cleanupInProgressRef.current) return;
116	    cleanupInProgressRef.current = true;
117	
118	    if (safetyTimerRef.current !== null) {
119	      globalThis.clearTimeout(safetyTimerRef.current);
120	      safetyTimerRef.current = null;
121	    }
122	
123	    ghostRef.current?.el.remove();
124	    ghostRef.current = null;
125	
126	    // Clear refs BEFORE releasePointerCapture so that any synchronous
127	    // lostpointercapture event triggered by it sees null refs and bails out.
128	    const el = capturedElRef.current;
129	    const pointerId = capturedPointerIdRef.current;
130	    capturedElRef.current = null;
131	    capturedPointerIdRef.current = null;
132	
133	    if (el !== null && pointerId !== null) {
134	      try {
135	        el.releasePointerCapture(pointerId);
136	      } catch {
137	        // Element may no longer be in the DOM — ignore.
138	      }
139	    }
140	
141	    isDragging.current = false;
142	    lastHoverZoneRef.current = null;
143	    lastHoverBankTileRef.current = null;
144	    startPos.current = null;
145	    cleanupInProgressRef.current = false;
146	  }, []);
147	
148	  const cleanup = useCallback(() => {
149	    cleanupGhost();
150	  }, [cleanupGhost]);
151	
152	  const onPointerDown = useCallback(
153	    (e: PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => {
154	      if (e.pointerType === 'mouse' || !tileId) return;
155	      e.currentTarget.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId);
156	      capturedElRef.current = e.currentTarget;
157	      capturedPointerIdRef.current = e.pointerId;
158	      startPos.current = { x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY };
159	      isDragging.current = false;
160	
161	      // When the captured element is removed from the DOM (e.g. a slot tile
162	      // gets auto-ejected while the user is dragging it), the browser fires
163	      // lostpointercapture and onPointerUp never fires.  Clean up here so the
164	      // ghost doesn't float for 5 seconds.
165	      e.currentTarget.addEventListener(
166	        'lostpointercapture',
167	        () => {
168	          if (!cleanupInProgressRef.current && isDragging.current) {
169	            cleanupGhost();
170	            onDragCancelRef.current?.();
171	          }
172	        },
173	        { once: true },
174	      );
175	    },
176	    [tileId, cleanupGhost],
177	  );
178	
179	  const onPointerMove = useCallback(
180	    (e: PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => {
181	      if (e.pointerType === 'mouse' || !startPos.current) return;
182	
183	      if (!isDragging.current) {
184	        const dx = e.clientX - startPos.current.x;
185	        const dy = e.clientY - startPos.current.y;
186	        if (
187	          Math.abs(dx) > DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX ||
188	          Math.abs(dy) > DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX
189	        ) {
190	          isDragging.current = true;
191	          ghostRef.current = buildGhost(
192	            e.currentTarget,
193	            label,
194	            e.clientX,
195	            e.clientY,
196	          );
197	          onDragStart?.();
198	
199	          safetyTimerRef.current = globalThis.setTimeout(() => {
200	            cleanupGhost();
201	            onDragCancelRef.current?.();
202	          }, 5000);
203	
204	          document.addEventListener('contextmenu', cleanupGhost, {
205	            once: true,
206	          });
207	          document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', cleanupGhost, {
208	            once: true,
209	          });
210	        }
211	      }
212	
213	      if (isDragging.current && ghostRef.current) {
214	        const { el, halfW, halfH } = ghostRef.current;
215	        el.style.left = `${e.clientX - halfW}px`;
216	        el.style.top = `${e.clientY - halfH}px`;
217	
218	        // Sample 5 points (pointer center + 4 ghost corners) so the drop zone
219	        // activates as soon as the ghost tile visually overlaps a slot.
220	        // Hide the ghost once before sampling so it doesn't block elementsFromPoint.
221	        el.style.visibility = 'hidden';
222	        const samplePoints: [number, number][] = [
223	          [e.clientX, e.clientY],
224	          [e.clientX - halfW, e.clientY - halfH],
225	          [e.clientX + halfW, e.clientY - halfH],
226	          [e.clientX - halfW, e.clientY + halfH],
227	          [e.clientX + halfW, e.clientY + halfH],
228	        ];
229	        let detectedZone: number | null = null;
230	        outer: for (const [px, py] of samplePoints) {
231	          for (const elem of document.elementsFromPoint(px, py)) {
232	            if (
233	              elem instanceof HTMLElement &&
234	              elem.dataset['zoneIndex'] !== undefined
235	            ) {
236	              const parsed = Number.parseInt(
237	                elem.dataset['zoneIndex'],
238	                10,
239	              );
240	              if (!Number.isNaN(parsed)) {
241	                detectedZone = parsed;
242	                break outer;
243	              }
244	            }
245	          }
246	        }
247	        el.style.visibility = 'visible';
248	        if (detectedZone !== lastHoverZoneRef.current) {
249	          lastHoverZoneRef.current = detectedZone;
250	          onHoverZoneRef.current?.(detectedZone);
251	        }
252	
253	        // Bank tile hover: detect which bank hole the ghost is over.
254	        if (onHoverBankTileRef.current) {
255	          let detectedBankTileId: string | null = null;
256	          if (detectedZone === null) {
257	            for (const [px, py] of samplePoints) {
258	              for (const elem of document.elementsFromPoint(px, py)) {
259	                if (
260	                  elem instanceof HTMLElement &&
261	                  elem.dataset['tileBankHole'] !== undefined
262	                ) {
263	                  detectedBankTileId =
264	                    elem.dataset['tileBankHole'] ?? null;
265	                  break;
266	                }
267	              }
268	              if (detectedBankTileId !== null) break;
269	            }
270	          }
271	          if (detectedBankTileId !== lastHoverBankTileRef.current) {
272	            lastHoverBankTileRef.current = detectedBankTileId;
273	            onHoverBankTileRef.current(detectedBankTileId);
274	          }
275	        }
276	      }
277	    },
278	    [label, onDragStart, cleanupGhost],
279	  );
280	
281	  const onPointerUp = useCallback(
282	    (e: PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => {
283	      if (e.pointerType === 'mouse' || !startPos.current) return;
284	
285	      if (isDragging.current) {
286	        // Capture ghost dimensions before removing.
287	        const ghostHalfW = ghostRef.current?.halfW ?? 0;
288	        const ghostHalfH = ghostRef.current?.halfH ?? 0;
289	
290	        // Remove ghost before elementsFromPoint so it doesn't interfere.
291	        ghostRef.current?.el.remove();
292	        ghostRef.current = null;
293	
294	        // Priority order (honours explicit pointer intent before ghost expansion):
295	        //   1. Center → zone   (precise slot aim)
296	        //   2. Center → bank   (explicit bank aim beats corner zone hits)
297	        //   3. Corners → zone  (expanded zone — ghost visually overlaps slot)
298	        //   4. Corners → bank  (expanded bank)
299	        //   5. Cancel
300	        const centerX = e.clientX;
301	        const centerY = e.clientY;
302	        const cornerPoints: [number, number][] = [
303	          [centerX - ghostHalfW, centerY - ghostHalfH],
304	          [centerX + ghostHalfW, centerY - ghostHalfH],
305	          [centerX - ghostHalfW, centerY + ghostHalfH],
306	          [centerX + ghostHalfW, centerY + ghostHalfH],
307	        ];
308	
309	        const findZoneAt = (px: number, py: number): number | null => {
310	          for (const el of document.elementsFromPoint(px, py)) {
311	            if (
312	              el instanceof HTMLElement &&
313	              el.dataset['zoneIndex'] !== undefined
314	            ) {
315	              const parsed = Number.parseInt(
316	                el.dataset['zoneIndex'],
317	                10,
318	              );
319	              if (!Number.isNaN(parsed)) return parsed;
320	            }
321	          }
322	          return null;
323	        };
324	        const findBankTileAt = (
325	          px: number,
326	          py: number,
327	        ): string | null => {
328	          for (const el of document.elementsFromPoint(px, py)) {
329	            if (
330	              el instanceof HTMLElement &&
331	              el.dataset['tileBankHole'] !== undefined
332	            ) {
333	              return el.dataset['tileBankHole'] ?? null;
334	            }
335	          }
336	          return null;
337	        };
338	        const isBankAt = (px: number, py: number): boolean =>
339	          document
340	            .elementsFromPoint(px, py)
341	            .some(
342	              (el) =>
343	                el instanceof HTMLElement &&
344	                el.dataset['tileBank'] !== undefined,
345	            );
346	
347	        // Priority order:
348	        //   1. Center → zone   (precise slot aim)
349	        //   2. Center → bank tile (specific hole — swap or return)
350	        //   3. Center → bank   (empty bank area — return to bank)
351	        //   4. Corners → zone  (expanded zone)
352	        //   5. Corners → bank tile
353	        //   6. Corners → bank
354	        //   7. Cancel
355	
356	        // 1. Center → zone
357	        const centerZone = findZoneAt(centerX, centerY);
358	        if (centerZone !== null) {
359	          onDrop(tileId, centerZone);
360	          cleanup();
361	          return;
362	        }
363	        // 2. Center → bank tile
364	        if (onDropOnBankTile) {
365	          const centerBankTile = findBankTileAt(centerX, centerY);
366	          if (centerBankTile !== null) {
367	            onDropOnBankTile(centerBankTile);
368	            cleanup();
369	            return;
370	          }
371	        }
372	        // 3. Center → bank
373	        if (isBankAt(centerX, centerY)) {
374	          onDropOnBank?.();
375	          cleanup();
376	          return;
377	        }
378	        // 4. Corners → zone
379	        let droppedZoneIndex: number | null = null;
380	        for (const [px, py] of cornerPoints) {
381	          const z = findZoneAt(px, py);
382	          if (z !== null) {
383	            droppedZoneIndex = z;
384	            break;
385	          }
386	        }
387	        if (droppedZoneIndex === null) {
388	          // 5. Corners → bank tile
389	          let cornerBankTile: string | null = null;
390	          if (onDropOnBankTile) {
391	            for (const [px, py] of cornerPoints) {
392	              const bt = findBankTileAt(px, py);
393	              if (bt !== null) {
394	                cornerBankTile = bt;
395	                break;
396	              }
397	            }
398	          }
399	          if (cornerBankTile !== null && onDropOnBankTile) {
400	            onDropOnBankTile(cornerBankTile);
401	          } else {
402	            // 6. Corners → bank
403	            let hitBank = false;
404	            for (const [px, py] of cornerPoints) {
405	              if (isBankAt(px, py)) {
406	                hitBank = true;
407	                break;
408	              }
409	            }
410	            if (hitBank) {
411	              onDropOnBank?.();
412	            } else {
413	              // 7. Cancel
414	              onDragCancelRef.current?.();
415	            }
416	          }
417	        } else {
418	          onDrop(tileId, droppedZoneIndex);
419	        }
420	      }
421	
422	      cleanup();
423	    },
424	    [tileId, onDrop, onDropOnBank, onDropOnBankTile, cleanup],
425	  );
426	
427	  const onPointerCancel = useCallback(
428	    (e: PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => {
429	      if (e.pointerType === 'mouse') return;
430	      if (isDragging.current) {
431	        onDragCancelRef.current?.();
432	      }
433	      cleanupGhost();
434	    },
435	    [cleanupGhost],
436	  );
437	
438	  return { onPointerDown, onPointerMove, onPointerUp, onPointerCancel };
439	};
440
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
278:{"body":"There are some users who are slightly dragging instead of tapping. I assume they are not used to touch devices yet, and they accidentally drag for a small number of pixels.\n\nSo we should consider a buffer of dragging very slightly as a tap, instead of dragging nowhere.\n\nWe can hear the sound of the letter when they do that (e.g. `c` for cat`). \n\nIf the buffer is not a proper solution, we could also add a setting to record their dragging movements to study why their tap is actually a drag.","comments":[],"labels":[],"title":"Feature/Bug: drag vs tap"}
291:{"body":"## Summary\n\nImplements the [fast-tap queue plan](docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-fast-tap-queue.md): fixes false error bounces on rapid Android taps by combining **bank pre-validation** with a module-scoped **pending placements** write-ahead log, plus a **`REJECT_TAP`** reducer path for retries without zone mutation.\n\n## Changes\n\n- **Types:** `REJECT_TAP` on `AnswerGameAction`; optional `expected` on `GameEvaluateEvent`.\n- **Reducer:** `REJECT_TAP` increments `retryCount` only.\n- **useTileEvaluation:** `placeTile` returns `{ correct }`; reject-mode wrong drag dispatches `REJECT_TAP`; all `game:evaluate` emits include `expected` where applicable.\n- **useAutoNextSlot:** `PlaceResult`, `clearPendingPlacements`, pending queue + pre-validation (skipped for `lock-manual`).\n- **useDraggableTile:** tap reject uses `data-shaking` throttle, shake + wrong styling/sound, `REJECT_TAP` + evaluate emit; touch drag reject uses `requestAnimationFrame` + bank shake styling.\n- **Docs:** `AnswerGame.flows.mdx` updated for tap path and `REJECT_TAP`.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `yarn typecheck`\n- `npx vitest run` (full suite)\n- Pre-push hooks (related unit + lint + typecheck) passed locally.\n\n## Local run\n\nFrom the worktree:\n\n```bash\ncd /Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/276-fast-tap-queue\nyarn install\nyarn dev\n```\n\nCloses #276.\n\n\n<!-- preview-urls -->\n---\n**Preview:** [App](https://leocaseiro.github.io/base-skill/pr/287/app/) · [Storybook](https://leocaseiro.github.io/base-skill/pr/287/docs/) · [Build details ↓](https://github.com/leocaseiro/base-skill/pull/287#issuecomment-)\n<!-- /preview-urls 304:{"body":"The user is tapping way to fast in the correct order, but the app has a delay to change the current slot, so they ended up frustrated.\n\nEg: for word cat\nUser tap real quick in the correct order, but when pressing a, after c, c is not yet in the correct slot, triggering an error bounce, instead of getting into a queue.\n\nWe should make a queue to solve this issue.","title":"Bug: side effect on checking the tapped answer, before its current slot"}

953:**Issue #298** is about users who accidentally drag slightly (a few pixels) when they intend to tap. The current `DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX = 8` in `useTouchDrag.ts` means once they move 8+ pixels, it's treated as a drag — and since they release near where they started (still over the bank), it fires `onDropOnBank` which does nothing. The tile speaks but never gets placed.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-02 03:32:32Z)**_

The `react/function-component-definition` rule applies to React components only, not generic hooks. The `useSettings` function uses `function` declaration style, but this is a hook, not a component. Let me also verify that `plainSettingsFromQuery` was already present in master:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-02 03:32:32Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Check the full original useSettings.ts to confirm function style was pre-existing

`git -C /Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/298-tap-forgiveness show origin/master:src/db/hooks/useSettings.ts 2>&1 | head -45`

```text
import { useMemo } from 'react';
import { EMPTY } from 'rxjs';
import { useRxDB } from './useRxDB';
import { useRxQuery } from './useRxQuery';
import type { SettingsDoc } from '@/db/schemas/settings';

const ANONYMOUS_PROFILE_ID = 'anonymous';
const ANONYMOUS_SETTINGS_ID = 'settings:anonymous';

const DEFAULT_SETTINGS: Omit<SettingsDoc, 'updatedAt'> = {
  id: ANONYMOUS_SETTINGS_ID,
  profileId: ANONYMOUS_PROFILE_ID,
  volume: 0.8,
  speechRate: 1,
  ttsEnabled: true,
  showSubtitles: true,
};

function plainSettingsFromQuery(
  doc: SettingsDoc | null | { toJSON: () => SettingsDoc },
): SettingsDoc | null {
  if (doc === null) {
    return null;
  }
  if (
    typeof (doc as { toJSON?: () => SettingsDoc }).toJSON === 'function'
  ) {
    return (doc as { toJSON: () => SettingsDoc }).toJSON();
  }
  return doc as SettingsDoc;
}

type UseSettingsResult = {
  settings: typeof DEFAULT_SETTINGS & Partial<SettingsDoc>;
  update: (patch: Partial<SettingsDoc>) => Promise<void>;
};

export function useSettings(): UseSettingsResult {
  const { db } = useRxDB();

  const query$ = useMemo(
    () => (db ? db.settings.findOne(ANONYMOUS_SETTINGS_ID).$ : EMPTY),
    [db],
  );
```
</details></tool-use>

950	
951	I've reviewed the issue and the relevant code. Here's what I see:
952	
953	**Issue #298** is about users who accidentally drag slightly (a few pixels) when they intend to tap. The current `DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX = 8` in `useTouchDrag.ts` means once they move 8+ pixels, it's treated as a drag — and since they release near where they started (still over the bank), it fires `onDropOnBank` which does nothing. The tile speaks but never gets placed.
954	
955	**PR #287** solved a different problem: fast sequential taps arriving before state updates (queueing). It didn't address accidental micro-drags.
956	
957	The fix space is clear and bounded — this is lightweight scope. Let me confirm my understanding with one question.
958	
959	_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 21:20:40Z)**_
960	
961	<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="ToolSearch"><details>
962	<summary>Tool use: **ToolSearch**</summary>
963	
964	**Input:**
965	
966	- max_results: `1`
967	- query: `select:AskUserQuestion`
968	
969	**Result:**
970	
971	```
972	
973	```
974	</details></tool-use>
975	
976	_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 21:21:07Z)**_
977	
978	_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 21:23:02Z)**_
979	
980	<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
981	<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
982	**When a user does a micro-drag (moves just slightly beyond the 8px threshold) and releases their finger still over the bank area — should that always be treated as a tap, or only when the total drag distance is short (to distinguish from a real drag-then-cancel-back-to-bank)?**
983	
984	Options:
985	- **Short drag → tap (Recommended)** - If total displacement from start is under ~20-30px AND the finger lifts over the bank, treat it as a tap (place in next slot). Longer drags that return to bank still cancel normally.
986	- **Any bank drop → tap** - Any time a drag ends over the bank (regardless of distance), treat it as a tap and place the tile. Simpler, but users who intentionally drag and change their mind can't cancel by returning to bank.
987	- **Just increase threshold** - Bump DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX from 8 to ~15-20px so micro-movements never trigger drag mode. Simple but makes real drags feel slightly less responsive.
988	
989	
990	**Answer:** I like 1, can we use time for that is overkill? Regardless of each threshold, make it available in the settings, so we can tweak with a real play user
991	</details></tool-use>
992	
993	_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 21:23:13Z)**_
994	
995	_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 21:23:15Z)**_
996	
997	Good call. Distance-based with a configurable threshold is the cleanest approach. Time *could* help (accidental micro-drags are fast, intentional drags are slower), but it adds complexity and distance alone should cover the problem — real drags travel far, accidental ones don't.
998	
999	Let me check where the game settings/config live so I know where the threshold would be exposed.
